description: A year-long fellowship engaging PhD students with classical political-economy thought through structured reading groups and mentorship from senior scholars.
eligibility: PhD students in economics, political science, philosophy, or a related field, from any country.
prepResources: Familiarity with classical political economy texts (Smith, Hayek, and similar) is directly tested in the application and interview.

title: Y Combinator Startup School
description: A free online course and community for early-stage founders, covering fundraising, product, and growth, with optional peer group matching for accountability.
eligibility: Open to anyone building or planning to build a startup, at any stage, worldwide.
prepResources: Y Combinator's own library of startup advice essays is the best complementary reading alongside the course videos.

title: MIT Solve The Care Economy Challenge
description: A challenge specifically for technology-enabled solutions to childcare, eldercare, and disability-support gaps, funded through MIT Solve's broader innovation network.
eligibility: Any individual or organization worldwide with a tech-enabled care-economy solution.
prepResources: MIT Solve evaluates on evidence of impact so far — even small-scale pilot data strengthens an early-stage application.

title: Kellogg-Morgan Stanley Sustainable Investing Challenge
org: Kellogg School of Management / Morgan Stanley
description: A graduate student pitch competition for innovative financial solutions to social and environmental problems, with a New York or London final pitch event.
eligibility: Graduate students (any discipline) working in teams of up to four.
prepResources: Past winning pitch decks are shared by Kellogg's Public-Private Interface — reviewing a few clarifies the level of financial rigor expected.
location: Global (online); final in New York or London

title: GCSP Prize for Transformative Futures in Peace and Security
description: A prize for innovative concepts addressing global peace and security challenges, with winners receiving a funded incubation period at GCSP in Geneva.
eligibility: Open to individuals or small teams worldwide with an original peace-and-security concept.
prepResources: GCSP's own publications on emerging security challenges are useful background for shaping a proposal that fits their strategic focus.

title: UNIDIR Global Disarmament Essay Competition
description: An essay competition inviting young people to write on disarmament, arms control, and international security, with a funded trip to Geneva for the winner.
eligibility: Ages roughly 19-29, from any country, submitting an original essay on disarmament themes.
prepResources: UNIDIR's own published research is the most directly relevant background reading for a competitive entry.

title: Non-Violent Communication Video Contest
description: A video contest inviting entrants to demonstrate nonviolent communication practices in everyday scenarios, with recognition and certificates for standout entries.
eligibility: Open to anyone worldwide, individually or in groups.
prepResources: CNVC's own training materials on the four-step NVC framework are the best reference for accurately depicting the practice.

title: Delhi Legislative Assembly Research Fellowship
description: A fellowship placing young people directly inside India's legislative process, supporting Members of the Delhi Assembly with policy research and drafting.
eligibility: Indian citizens aged roughly 21-35 with a bachelor's degree, willing to be based in Delhi.
prepResources: A demonstrated interest in public policy or governance (coursework, prior internships) is more relevant here than a purely academic profile.

title: LAMP Fellowship
org: Legislative Assistants to Members of Parliament (LAMP)
description: A fellowship placing young graduates as research assistants to sitting Indian Members of Parliament for a year, providing direct exposure to national policymaking.
eligibility: Indian citizens under 25 who have completed an undergraduate degree.
prepResources: Strong written research and analysis samples are central to the application, since the role is fundamentally about policy research support.

title: Punjab Good Governance Fellowship Program
org: Government of Punjab / Indian School of Business
description: A fellowship placing graduates as Governance or District Development Fellows within the Punjab state government, combined with structured training at the Indian School of Business.
eligibility: Indian citizens with a bachelor's degree and strong interest in public administration or development work.
prepResources: Prior exposure to government or public-sector work (internships, coursework) strengthens the application significantly.

title: AIF Banyan Impact Fellowship
description: A fellowship placing young professionals with development organizations across India and the US to build cross-border development leadership skills.
eligibility: Ages roughly 21-35 with a bachelor's degree, from India, the US, or the diaspora.
prepResources: Prior volunteer or professional experience with a development-sector organization strengthens an application meaningfully.

title: India Health Policy and System Research Fellowship
org: Sir Ratan Tata Trust / Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation
description: A fellowship training graduates in rigorous Health Policy and Systems Research methods through mentored study on a real health-system problem in India.
eligibility: College graduates in public health, medicine, social sciences, or a related field, based in India.
prepResources: Familiarity with basic health-systems research methods (case studies, mixed methods) is useful preparation for the interview.

title: Reporters Without Borders Digital Security Scholarship Program
description: A program sending journalists from conflict or high-risk zones to Berlin for intensive digital-security and safety training, with a living stipend during the program.
eligibility: Working journalists, particularly those reporting from conflict zones or under press-freedom threats.
prepResources: A clear account of the specific security risks you face in your reporting work is central to a strong application.

title: Reuters Institute Journalist Fellowship (Oxford)
org: Reuters Institute for the Study of Journalism, University of Oxford
description: A fellowship giving working journalists three to six months at Oxford to research a topic of their choice in journalism practice, combined with academic seminars.
eligibility: Practicing journalists with several years of professional experience, from any country.
prepResources: A tightly scoped research question about journalism practice — not a general interest area — is what the selection committee looks for.

title: Obama Foundation Leaders Program
description: A leadership development program for civic and community leaders across regions (Africa, Asia-Pacific, USA, Europe), combining in-person convenings with ongoing skills training.
eligibility: Civic leaders roughly aged 25-45 with a demonstrated track record of community impact in their region.
prepResources: The program looks for leaders already running real initiatives — concrete outcomes from your existing work matter more than potential.

title: Acumen India Fellowship
description: A year-long leadership fellowship for India-based professionals working on poverty-related challenges, combining moral leadership training with real project work.
eligibility: India-based professionals already working on a social-impact initiative or organization.
prepResources: A concrete, ongoing social-impact project (not just intent to start one) is central to a competitive application.

title: Young Social Entrepreneurs Programme
description: An eight-month program helping young people start or scale social enterprises, combining mentorship, cross-border study visits, and seed funding for top proposals.
eligibility: Ages 18-35 from any country with an early-stage social enterprise idea or existing venture.
prepResources: A specific, locally grounded social problem and a concrete business model (not just a mission statement) strengthens the application.

title: Youth Co:Lab National Innovation Challenge
description: A regional startup idea challenge for young people building solutions to social problems such as education access, care work, and disability inclusion, with seed funding for finalists.
eligibility: Ages 18-29, from participating countries in Asia-Pacific, with an early-stage social venture idea.
prepResources: Youth Co:Lab runs through national UNDP offices — check your country's local chapter for the current cycle's exact criteria.

title: IFC Impact Investing Challenge
description: A team-based challenge for graduate students to develop investment strategies addressing climate, gender equity, or financial inclusion, culminating in a pitch to IFC leadership.
eligibility: Graduate students worldwide, working in teams of three to five.
prepResources: The IFC's own sector reports on impact investing are useful grounding for building a credible, data-backed investment thesis.

title: Visa Everywhere Initiative
description: A global startup competition supporting founders building innovative payment and commerce technologies, with cash prizes and mentorship from Visa's innovation team.
eligibility: Early-stage fintech startups worldwide, applying by region.
prepResources: Visa runs regional heats before the global final — check your region's specific timeline and requirements on the program site.

title: MIT Solve Financial Inclusion Challenge
description: A challenge for solutions expanding financial access to excluded individuals and small enterprises, with winners receiving funding and MIT Solve's mentorship network.
eligibility: Any individual or organization worldwide with a financial-inclusion solution.
prepResources: MIT Solve looks for evidence the solution already works at small scale — pilot data strengthens even an early application.

title: RBI Harbinger: Innovation for Transformation Hackathon
description: India's central bank hackathon inviting teams to build fintech solutions across themes like fraud prevention and green finance, with one of the largest cash prize pools in Indian hackathons.
eligibility: Individuals or teams aged 18 and older, from any country, though most participants are Indian fintech developers.
prepResources: The RBI publishes detailed problem statements per theme months ahead — reviewing a past year's winning submissions clarifies expected technical depth.

title: EY Techathon
description: A student hackathon challenging teams to apply emerging technology like AI and automation to real business and community problems across several themed tracks.
eligibility: Teams of Indian college students, typically up to five members per team.
prepResources: EY publishes the year's themed tracks in advance — picking a track matching your team's existing technical strengths is important given short build windows.

title: Reply Cyber Security Challenge
description: A 24-hour team cybersecurity competition spanning categories like cryptography, web security, and reverse engineering, judged on a capture-the-flag scoring system.
eligibility: Individuals or teams of two to four, aged 16 and older, from any country.
prepResources: Practicing on general capture-the-flag platforms (which mirror this format) is the most direct preparation.

title: ANU Science Case Study Competition
description: A case-study competition for undergraduate teams analyzing a real scientific or policy problem (such as critical minerals or mental health), judged on rigor and communication.
eligibility: Undergraduate students worldwide, working in teams of two to four.
prepResources: ANU releases the specific case topic and dataset ahead of the deadline — grounding the analysis in that provided data is essential.

title: Kokuyo Design Award
description: An annual stationery, furniture, and everyday-tool design competition open globally, judged on originality and practical usability, with a substantial cash grand prize.
eligibility: Open to designers and students of any nationality; entries are typically concept sketches or prototypes.
prepResources: Kokuyo sets a specific annual theme and publishes past winning designs — matching the brief's practical usability bar matters most.

title: Loewe Foundation Craft Prize
description: An international craft prize recognizing exceptional handmade work across disciplines like ceramics, textiles, and woodworking, with exhibition and a substantial cash prize.
eligibility: Practicing craftspeople and artisans of any nationality, submitting a single completed work.
prepResources: The Prize favors technical mastery combined with innovation — past winners' work (viewable on the site) illustrates the expected caliber.
location: Global (online submission); exhibition varies by year

title: AICTE Pragati Scholarship for Girl Students
org: All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE)
description: A government scholarship supporting girl students pursuing technical undergraduate education, aimed at improving female representation in engineering fields.
eligibility: Female students admitted to an AICTE-approved technical degree program in India, with family income limits.
prepResources: Applications run through the National Scholarship Portal — keep your Aadhaar and income certificate ready before the window opens.

title: AICTE Swanath Scholarship
org: All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE)
description: A scholarship for orphans and dependents of deceased or disabled armed forces personnel pursuing technical higher education in India.
eligibility: Students in India who are orphans or dependents of deceased/disabled defense personnel, admitted to a technical program.
prepResources: Documentation proving the eligible family circumstance is the most time-consuming part — gather it well before the deadline.

title: LIC HFL Vidyadhan Scholarship
description: A scholarship for meritorious students from economically weaker sections of Indian society, covering school and college education costs.
eligibility: School and college students in India with strong academic marks and family income below a set threshold.
prepResources: The scholarship weighs prior academic performance heavily — have your most recent mark sheets ready for upload.

title: Central Sector Scheme of Scholarships for College and University Students
org: Ministry of Education, Government of India
description: A national merit-based scholarship for Indian students from lower-income families entering college, renewable annually based on continued academic performance.
eligibility: Indian students who scored highly on their class 12 board exams, from families below a defined income threshold.
prepResources: All applications route through the National Scholarship Portal — registering early avoids last-minute portal congestion near the deadline.

title: JK Laxmipat Vidya Scholarship
description: A scholarship for economically disadvantaged school and college students in India, awarded based on merit and family financial need.
eligibility: School or college students in India with family income below a defined threshold and strong academic marks.
prepResources: Income certificates and recent academic transcripts are the two documents most commonly required at application.

title: Ericsson Empowering Girlchild Scholarship
description: A scholarship for underprivileged girl students pursuing engineering (IT/CS) or MBA programs in India, aimed at improving gender diversity in technology careers.
eligibility: Female college students in India enrolled in an engineering or MBA program, from lower-income households.
prepResources: Strong academic transcripts and a clear articulation of career goals in technology are the main application components.

title: Siemens Scholarship Program
description: A four-year scholarship for meritorious engineering students in India from low-income families, covering tuition and a living allowance.
eligibility: Students below age 20 admitted to an engineering program in India, with family income below a defined threshold.
prepResources: Strong class 12 marks in physics, chemistry, and math are typically weighted heavily in the selection process.

title: Sashakt Scholarship for Women in Science
description: A scholarship supporting rural, economically disadvantaged girls pursuing a Bachelor of Science degree in India, often paired with mentorship from women scientists.
eligibility: Female class 12 graduates in India from rural or low-income backgrounds admitted to a BSc program.
prepResources: Highlighting any prior exposure to science fairs, labs, or STEM extracurriculars strengthens the mentorship-matching component.

title: Colgate Keep India Smiling Scholarship Program
description: A scholarship combined with mentorship for economically weaker school and college students in India, spanning educational, sports, and general-merit categories.
eligibility: School or college students in India from lower-income households, applying under one of three eligibility categories.
prepResources: Choose the category (academic, sports, or general) that best fits your actual achievements, since criteria differ meaningfully between them.

title: Ashton Award for Student Research
description: A research grant supporting undergraduate and graduate student fieldwork on Asian tropical forest biology, tenable at field sites across Asia.
eligibility: Undergraduate or graduate students proposing original field research on Asian tropical forest ecosystems.
prepResources: A specific, feasible field research plan with a named site and methodology is essential — vague proposals are not competitive.

title: UPSC Civil Services Examination
description: India's premier annual recruitment exam for the IAS, IPS, IFS, and other central civil services, conducted in three stages: preliminary, main, and interview.
eligibility: Indian citizens with a bachelor's degree, generally aged 21-32 (with relaxations for reserved categories), limited number of attempts.
prepResources: UPSC publishes the full syllabus and past years' question papers on its site — most candidates prepare over 12-18 months given the exam's breadth.

title: SSC Combined Graduate Level (CGL) Examination
description: An annual nationwide exam recruiting graduates into a wide range of Group B and C posts across central government ministries and departments.
eligibility: Indian citizens with a bachelor's degree, generally aged 18-32 depending on the specific post.
prepResources: SSC releases the tier-wise syllabus and previous papers on its site; the exam runs in multiple tiers (objective, descriptive, skill test depending on post).

title: SSC Combined Higher Secondary Level (CHSL) Examination
description: An annual recruitment exam for Lower Divisional Clerk, Postal Assistant, and Data Entry Operator posts across central government offices, open to school-leaving candidates.
eligibility: Indian citizens who have completed class 12, generally aged 18-27.
prepResources: The exam has objective, descriptive, and typing/skill test stages — SSC's official notification each year details the exact pattern.

title: IBPS Probationary Officer (PO) Recruitment
description: A common recruitment process used by most Indian public sector banks to hire Probationary Officers, run as a three-stage exam: preliminary, main, and interview.
eligibility: Indian citizens with a bachelor's degree in any discipline, generally aged 20-30.
prepResources: IBPS publishes the syllabus for each stage; quantitative aptitude, reasoning, and English are consistently tested across the exam series.

title: IBPS Clerk Recruitment
description: A common recruitment process for clerical-cadre positions across most Indian public sector banks, conducted through a two-stage online exam.
eligibility: Indian citizens with a bachelor's degree in any discipline, generally aged 20-28.
prepResources: Preliminary and main exam syllabi are published by IBPS annually, along with prior years' cut-off scores by state.

title: State Bank of India Probationary Officer (SBI PO) Recruitment
description: SBI's own recruitment process for Probationary Officers, India's largest public sector bank, run as a three-stage exam separate from the IBPS common process.
eligibility: Indian citizens with a bachelor's degree in any discipline, generally aged 21-30.
prepResources: SBI releases its own specific exam pattern and previous papers on its careers portal, distinct from the IBPS pattern.

title: State Bank of India Clerk (Junior Associate) Recruitment
description: SBI's own recruitment process for clerical-cadre Junior Associate positions, run as a two-stage exam with a large number of vacancies each cycle.
eligibility: Indian citizens with a bachelor's degree in any discipline, generally aged 20-28.
prepResources: SBI's careers portal publishes the current cycle's notification, exam pattern, and previous cut-offs by state cadre.

title: RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment
description: A highly competitive recruitment exam for Grade B officer positions at India's central bank, spanning general, DEPR, and DSIM specialist streams.
eligibility: Indian citizens with a bachelor's degree (or specific postgraduate qualifications for specialist streams), generally aged 21-30.
prepResources: RBI publishes its own detailed syllabus by phase and stream; the DEPR/DSIM streams require strong economics or statistics backgrounds respectively.

title: RBI Assistant Recruitment
description: A recruitment exam for clerical-cadre Assistant positions at the Reserve Bank of India, run as a two-stage online exam plus a language proficiency test.
eligibility: Indian citizens with a bachelor's degree in any discipline, generally aged 20-28.
prepResources: RBI publishes the current notification and past cut-offs on its careers page each recruitment cycle.

title: Railway Recruitment Board NTPC Examination
description: A large-scale recruitment exam for non-technical popular categories (station master, clerk, ticket examiner, and similar roles) across Indian Railways zones.
eligibility: Indian citizens with a bachelor's degree (graduate-level posts) or class 12 (undergraduate-level posts), generally aged 18-33.
prepResources: Each of the 21 zonal RRBs coordinates hiring together under a common exam — check your nearest zone's RRB site for the current notification.

title: Railway Recruitment Board Group D Examination
description: One of India's largest recruitment drives, hiring for entry-level technical and support roles (track maintainer, helper, porter, and similar) across Indian Railways.
eligibility: Indian citizens who have completed class 10 or an ITI course, generally aged 18-33.
prepResources: The exam includes a computer-based test, physical efficiency test, and document verification — RRB publishes the exact pattern per cycle.

title: Indian Army Agniveer Recruitment
description: India's short-service enlistment scheme for soldiers across combat and technical roles, combining a physical fitness test, medical exam, and written test.
eligibility: Indian citizens who have completed class 10 or 12 (depending on stream), generally aged 17.5-21.
prepResources: The Army publishes physical fitness standards and syllabus by entry stream on its recruitment site well ahead of each rally.

title: Indian Navy Agniveer and Officer Entry Recruitment
description: Recruitment pathways into the Indian Navy spanning short-service Agniveer enlistment and officer entries through NDA, CDS, and direct-entry schemes.
eligibility: Indian citizens who have completed class 10, 12, or a bachelor's degree depending on the entry scheme, with specific age limits per scheme.
prepResources: The Navy's recruitment site breaks down each entry scheme's exact eligibility and exam pattern — matching your qualification level to the right scheme is the first step.

title: Indian Air Force Agniveer and Officer Entry Recruitment
description: Recruitment pathways into the Indian Air Force spanning short-service Agniveer Vayu enlistment and officer entries through NDA, AFCAT, and technical branches.
eligibility: Indian citizens who have completed class 10, 12, or a bachelor's degree depending on the entry scheme, with specific age limits per scheme.
prepResources: AFCAT (for officer entries) and the Agniveer Vayu written exam each have separate published syllabi on the recruitment site.

title: Indian Coast Guard Recruitment
description: Recruitment into the Indian Coast Guard across Navik (general duty and domestic branch) and Assistant Commandant officer entries.
eligibility: Indian citizens who have completed class 10, 12, or a bachelor's degree depending on the entry level, with specific age limits per post.
prepResources: The Coast Guard's recruitment portal lists physical standards and exam syllabus separately for each entry category.

title: CRPF Constable and Officer Recruitment
description: Recruitment into India's largest paramilitary force across constable, sub-inspector, and officer-level posts through SSC-coordinated and direct exams.
eligibility: Indian citizens who have completed class 10, 12, or a bachelor's degree depending on the post, with specific age and physical standards.
prepResources: Constable-level recruitment for CRPF typically runs through the SSC GD Constable exam — check both the CRPF and SSC sites for current cycles.

title: Border Security Force (BSF) Recruitment
description: Recruitment into India's primary border-guarding force across constable, sub-inspector, and officer-level posts.
eligibility: Indian citizens who have completed class 10, 12, or a bachelor's degree depending on the post, with specific age and physical standards.
prepResources: BSF's own recruitment portal lists post-wise eligibility and physical standards; constable-level hiring often coordinates with the SSC GD exam.

title: Central Industrial Security Force (CISF) Recruitment
description: Recruitment into the force responsible for securing India's critical infrastructure (airports, PSUs, nuclear facilities), across constable and officer-level posts.
eligibility: Indian citizens who have completed class 10, 12, or a bachelor's degree depending on the post, with specific age and physical standards.
prepResources: Constable-level recruitment typically runs through the SSC GD Constable exam — check the CISF site for direct officer-entry notifications.

title: ISRO Scientist/Engineer Recruitment
description: Recruitment of engineering and science graduates into ISRO's scientist and engineer cadre, hiring through a written exam or, for top engineering graduates, GATE scores.
eligibility: Bachelor's degree in an engineering discipline relevant to ISRO's work (aerospace, mechanical, electronics, computer science, etc.), with defined marks cutoffs.
prepResources: ISRO's Centralized Recruitment Board publishes the exact syllabus and eligible disciplines for each cycle on the ISRO careers page.

title: DRDO Scientist Recruitment (CEPTAM)
description: Recruitment of science and engineering graduates into DRDO's scientist cadre through the Centralised Entry Scheme for Technical Assistants and direct scientist entries.
eligibility: Bachelor's, master's, or PhD in a relevant science or engineering discipline, depending on the specific entry level.
prepResources: DRDO's careers page details the exact entry schemes (CEPTAM, direct scientist recruitment via GATE) and their separate eligibility criteria.

title: Bhabha Atomic Research Centre Training Scheme (OCES/DGFS)
description: A one-year paid training program for engineering and science graduates leading to a scientific officer role within India's Department of Atomic Energy.
eligibility: Bachelor's degree in engineering or a master's in a physical science, meeting BARC's specific marks and GATE-score criteria.
prepResources: BARC's own OCES/DGFS scheme has a distinct written exam and interview process separate from general PSU recruitment — the syllabus is published on the careers page.

title: NTPC Engineering Executive Trainee Recruitment
description: A graduate engineer trainee program at India's largest power generation company, hiring primarily through GATE scores followed by an interview.
eligibility: Bachelor's degree in engineering with a qualifying GATE score in the relevant discipline.
prepResources: NTPC publishes its GATE-score cutoffs by discipline from prior years, which is the clearest signal of competitiveness for a given branch.

title: ONGC Graduate Trainee Recruitment
description: A graduate engineer and geoscientist trainee program at India's largest oil and gas exploration company, hiring primarily through GATE scores.
eligibility: Bachelor's degree in engineering or geosciences with a qualifying GATE score in the relevant discipline.
prepResources: ONGC's careers page lists eligible disciplines and prior years' GATE cutoffs; petroleum, mechanical, and geophysics streams are consistently in demand.

title: Indian Oil Corporation Engineer/Officer Trainee Recruitment
description: A graduate engineer and management trainee program at India's largest oil refining and marketing company, hiring primarily through GATE scores or a dedicated recruitment exam.
eligibility: Bachelor's degree in engineering (or an MBA for management trainee roles) with a qualifying GATE score or exam performance.
prepResources: IOCL runs both GATE-based and non-GATE recruitment cycles depending on the year — check the current notification for the applicable route.

title: GAIL Executive Trainee Recruitment
description: A graduate engineer trainee program at India's leading natural gas processing and distribution company, hiring primarily through GATE scores.
eligibility: Bachelor's degree in engineering with a qualifying GATE score in the relevant discipline (mechanical, chemical, electrical, instrumentation, or civil).
prepResources: GAIL's careers page lists eligible GATE papers and disciplines by cycle — chemical and mechanical branches are consistently recruited.

title: Hindustan Petroleum Corporation Officer Trainee Recruitment
description: A graduate engineer and management trainee program at one of India's major public sector oil refining and marketing companies, hiring via GATE scores and dedicated exams.
eligibility: Bachelor's degree in engineering (or an MBA for management trainee roles) meeting HPCL's specific eligibility criteria.
prepResources: HPCL's careers page details the current cycle's exact route (GATE-based or company exam) and eligible disciplines.

title: Steel Authority of India Management Trainee Recruitment
description: A graduate engineer and management trainee program at India's largest state-owned steel producer, hiring primarily through GATE scores and management aptitude tests.
eligibility: Bachelor's degree in engineering (technical trainee) or any discipline plus management aptitude (management trainee), with GATE or exam-based qualification.
prepResources: SAIL's careers portal lists the specific route and eligible disciplines for the current recruitment cycle.

title: Bharat Heavy Electricals Limited (BHEL) Engineer Trainee Recruitment
description: A graduate engineer trainee program at India's largest power and industrial equipment manufacturer, hiring primarily through GATE scores.
eligibility: Bachelor's degree in engineering with a qualifying GATE score in mechanical, electrical, electronics, or a related discipline.
prepResources: BHEL's careers page lists eligible GATE papers and prior years' cutoff scores by discipline.

title: Coal India Management Trainee Recruitment
description: A graduate engineer and management trainee program at the world's largest coal-mining company, hiring primarily through GATE scores or a dedicated recruitment exam.
eligibility: Bachelor's degree in engineering (mining, mechanical, electrical, and others) meeting Coal India's specific criteria.
prepResources: Coal India's careers page details the current cycle's exact eligibility and disciplines recruited across its subsidiary companies.

title: Power Grid Corporation Executive Trainee Recruitment
org: Power Grid Corporation of India Limited (PGCIL)
description: A graduate engineer trainee program at India's central power transmission utility, hiring primarily through GATE scores.
eligibility: Bachelor's degree in engineering with a qualifying GATE score in electrical, civil, or a related discipline.
prepResources: PGCIL's careers page lists eligible GATE papers and prior years' cutoff scores by discipline.

title: Bharat Electronics Limited (BEL) Engineer Recruitment
description: A graduate engineer trainee program at India's leading defense electronics manufacturer, hiring primarily through GATE scores or a dedicated recruitment exam.
eligibility: Bachelor's degree in electronics, computer science, or a related engineering discipline meeting BEL's specific criteria.
prepResources: BEL's careers page lists the current cycle's exact route and eligible engineering disciplines.

title: NABARD Grade A/B Officer Recruitment
org: National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development (NABARD)
description: A recruitment exam for officer-cadre positions at India's apex rural development bank, spanning general and specialist streams like agriculture and rural development.
eligibility: Bachelor's degree in a relevant discipline (varying by stream), generally aged 21-30.
prepResources: NABARD's own exam pattern (preliminary, main, interview) and prior cutoffs by stream are published on the careers page.

title: Export-Import Bank of India Management Trainee Recruitment
description: A management trainee program at India's export credit agency, recruiting graduates and postgraduates into roles supporting international trade finance.
eligibility: Bachelor's or master's degree in a relevant discipline (finance, economics, or management), meeting EXIM Bank's specific criteria.
prepResources: EXIM Bank's careers page details the current cycle's exam pattern and eligible qualifications.

title: AIIMS Recruitment (Faculty, Nursing, and Non-Teaching Posts)
org: All India Institute of Medical Sciences (AIIMS)
description: Recruitment across India's premier medical institutions for faculty, nursing officer, and non-teaching administrative positions.
eligibility: Relevant medical, nursing, or administrative qualification depending on the specific post advertised.
prepResources: Each AIIMS campus and the central exam body release separate notifications — check the specific campus's recruitment page for open posts.

title: Employees' State Insurance Corporation (ESIC) Recruitment
description: Recruitment across ESIC's medical, nursing, and administrative cadres, supporting India's social security and health insurance system for workers.
eligibility: Relevant medical, nursing, or administrative qualification depending on the specific post advertised.
prepResources: ESIC releases post-specific notifications — some posts (like Upper Division Clerk) route through the SSC exam rather than a direct ESIC exam.

title: National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) Recruitment
description: Recruitment of engineers and management professionals into India's national highway development and management authority.
eligibility: Bachelor's degree in civil engineering or a related field, or an MBA for management roles, depending on the post.
prepResources: NHAI publishes post-specific eligibility and exam details on its recruitment page each cycle.

title: Bureau of Indian Standards (BIS) Recruitment
description: Recruitment across scientific, technical, and administrative posts at India's national standards body, spanning multiple engineering and science disciplines.
eligibility: Bachelor's or master's degree in a relevant science, engineering, or administrative field depending on the post.
prepResources: BIS releases exam patterns per post category on its vacancies page — technical posts typically require a discipline-specific written test.

title: Sports Authority of India (SAI) Recruitment
description: Recruitment of coaches, sports scientists, and administrative staff into India's national sports development body.
eligibility: Relevant sports science, coaching diploma, or administrative qualification depending on the post advertised.
prepResources: SAI's recruitment notifications are post-specific — coaching roles typically require a National Institute of Sports diploma or equivalent.

title: Centre for Development of Advanced Computing (C-DAC) Recruitment
org: Centre for Development of Advanced Computing (C-DAC)
description: Recruitment of engineers and scientists into India's premier R&D organization for high-performance computing, AI, and electronics.
eligibility: Bachelor's or master's degree in computer science, electronics, or a related engineering discipline.
prepResources: C-DAC's careers page lists project-specific vacancies and eligibility, since many roles are tied to specific funded R&D projects.

title: National Institute of Electronics and Information Technology (NIELIT) Recruitment
org: National Institute of Electronics and Information Technology
description: Recruitment of scientific and technical staff into India's national IT training and R&D institute across its regional centers.
eligibility: Bachelor's or master's degree in computer science, electronics, or IT, depending on the post advertised.
prepResources: NIELIT posts vacancies by regional center — checking the specific center relevant to your location is useful alongside the national listing.

title: Bharat Sanchar Nigam Limited (BSNL) Recruitment
description: Recruitment of engineers and technical staff into India's state-owned telecommunications company, spanning junior telecom officer and other technical posts.
eligibility: Bachelor's degree in electronics, telecommunications, or a related engineering discipline, depending on the post.
prepResources: BSNL's careers page lists post-specific eligibility and exam details for the current recruitment cycle.

title: Airports Authority of India (AAI) Recruitment
description: Recruitment of engineers, air traffic control officers, and management trainees into India's national airport operating authority.
eligibility: Bachelor's degree in engineering (junior executive posts) or a relevant discipline for ATC/management roles, with specific criteria per post.
prepResources: AAI's careers page details the exam pattern separately for Junior Executive (engineering), ATC, and management trainee tracks.

title: Life Insurance Corporation (LIC) Assistant Administrative Officer Recruitment
description: A recruitment exam for Assistant Administrative Officer positions at India's largest public sector insurer, spanning generalist and specialist streams.
eligibility: Bachelor's degree in any discipline (or a relevant specialist qualification for specialist streams), generally aged 21-30.
prepResources: LIC's own exam pattern (preliminary, main, interview) and prior cutoffs are published on its careers page each cycle.

title: Employees' Provident Fund Organisation (EPFO) Recruitment
description: Recruitment for Assistant Provident Fund Commissioner and other posts managing India's retirement savings and social security schemes for salaried workers.
eligibility: Bachelor's degree in any discipline, generally aged 21-30, depending on the post.
prepResources: EPFO's recruitment notifications are released periodically — the Assistant Provident Fund Commissioner exam is UPSC-coordinated.

title: Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program
description: A large-scale scholarship covering full undergraduate or graduate study costs for academically talented young Africans, delivered through a network of partner universities across Africa, Canada, and beyond.
eligibility: Young people from Sub-Saharan Africa facing significant financial or social barriers to education, admitted to a Mastercard Foundation partner university.
prepResources: Applications route through specific partner universities rather than a single central portal — check which partner institutions currently host the program.

title: Mandela Rhodes Scholarship
description: A postgraduate scholarship for African leaders combining full funding for study in South Africa with a structured leadership development program.
eligibility: African citizens with an undergraduate degree, admitted to or applying for postgraduate study at a South African university.
prepResources: The Foundation emphasizes leadership potential as much as academics — prior community leadership experience strengthens the application.

title: Tony Elumelu Foundation Entrepreneurship Programme
description: A pan-African entrepreneurship program providing seed capital, mentorship, and business training to early-stage African founders across every sector and all 54 countries.
eligibility: African citizens aged 18 and older with a business idea or an early-stage business based in Africa.
prepResources: The application includes a structured business plan template on the Foundation's own platform — completing it fully and specifically matters more than polish.

title: Mandela Washington Fellowship for Young African Leaders (YALI)
org: U.S. Department of State / Young African Leaders Initiative
description: A fully funded six-week leadership institute in the United States for young African leaders across business, civic, and public management tracks.
eligibility: African citizens aged 25-35 with a demonstrated record of leadership and community impact.
prepResources: The application asks for specific, measurable examples of leadership impact — concrete outcomes matter more than job titles.

title: African Leadership Academy Scholars Program
description: A two-year pre-university leadership and entrepreneurship program in South Africa for exceptional African teenagers, with need-based financial aid covering most costs.
eligibility: African students roughly aged 15-19 completing secondary school, with strong academic and leadership potential.
prepResources: The admissions process includes an entrepreneurial-leadership assessment alongside academics — prior youth leadership or business projects help.

title: Anzisha Prize
description: A pan-African prize recognizing young entrepreneurs building impactful, job-creating businesses, with cash prizes and an entrepreneurship fellowship for finalists.
eligibility: African entrepreneurs aged 15-22 running an operating business for at least one year.
prepResources: Anzisha evaluates on real business traction (revenue, jobs created) rather than concept alone — have concrete numbers ready.

title: Next Einstein Forum Fellowship
org: Next Einstein Forum / African Institute for Mathematical Sciences
description: A fellowship recognizing outstanding young African scientists across all disciplines, providing a platform to showcase research and connect with global science leaders.
eligibility: African scientists under 42 years old with a strong record of scientific research and public engagement.
prepResources: Selection weighs both research quality and demonstrated science-communication ability — a public-facing science project strengthens the profile.

title: African Institute for Mathematical Sciences (AIMS) Master's Program
description: A fully funded one-year intensive master's program in mathematical sciences across AIMS centers in six African countries, taught by visiting international faculty.
eligibility: African students with a strong undergraduate degree in mathematics, physics, computer science, or engineering.
prepResources: Strong quantitative fundamentals from an undergraduate degree matter more than a specific institution's prestige — AIMS looks broadly across Africa for talent.
location: Multiple African countries (South Africa, Senegal, Ghana, Cameroon, Rwanda, Tanzania)

title: Miles Morland Writing Scholarship
description: A six-month scholarship supporting African writers to complete a book-length work of fiction or non-fiction, with a stipend covering living costs during the writing period.
eligibility: African citizens who have not previously published a book, with a substantial writing sample and clear project plan.
prepResources: A detailed synopsis and a strong sample chapter matter more than a finished manuscript — the scholarship is meant to fund unfinished work.

title: Africa's Business Heroes Prize Competition
description: A pan-African entrepreneurship competition recognizing outstanding African founders across sectors, with a substantial cash prize pool and mentorship from global business leaders.
eligibility: African entrepreneurs running an operating business with real revenue or measurable impact.
prepResources: The competition emphasizes both business viability and social impact — a clear metric-backed pitch deck is central to advancing past early rounds.

title: Camfed Learner Guide and Scholarship Program
description: A scholarship and mentorship program supporting girls in rural Sub-Saharan Africa to complete secondary school, paired with a peer-mentoring network run by young female graduates.
eligibility: Girls in rural, low-income communities across Camfed's partner countries (Zambia, Zimbabwe, Ghana, Tanzania, Malawi) at risk of dropping out of school.
prepResources: Camfed operates through local partner schools and district offices — connecting with the district education office is the practical first step.

title: Ashinaga Africa Initiative Scholarship
description: A fully funded scholarship covering university study abroad (including in the US, UK, and Japan) for African students who have lost one or both parents.
eligibility: African students who have lost one or both parents, with strong academic performance and university admission ambitions.
prepResources: Candidates go through a residential preparatory program before study abroad — being ready to commit to a multi-year structured program matters.

title: MEST Africa Entrepreneur-in-Training Program
org: MEST Africa (Meltwater Entrepreneurial School of Technology)
description: A fully funded one-year training program teaching software development, business, and communications skills to aspiring African tech entrepreneurs, with seed funding for top graduating teams.
eligibility: African university graduates or young professionals with an interest in building a technology startup.
prepResources: Basic coding literacy is helpful but not required going in — MEST trains from fundamentals through a structured year-long curriculum.
location: Ghana, Nigeria, South Africa, Kenya (multiple hubs)

title: African Union Youth Volunteer Corps
description: A continental volunteer program placing young African professionals in year-long service assignments across AU member states to build pan-African development capacity.
eligibility: African citizens aged 18-35 with a bachelor's degree, willing to relocate to a different AU member state for the assignment.
prepResources: Placements are matched to specific skill needs across member states — a clear professional specialization strengthens the application.

title: Google for Startups Accelerator: Latin America
description: A three-month equity-free accelerator for Latin American tech startups using AI and cloud technology, with Google mentorship, credits, and a demo day.
eligibility: Early-stage tech startups based in Latin America with a working product or prototype.
prepResources: Google for Startups publishes past cohort companies — reviewing them clarifies the stage and sector focus for the current cycle.

title: Kiva Fellows Program
description: A volunteer fellowship placing early-career professionals with microfinance institutions in Latin America, Africa, and Asia to strengthen impact measurement and operations.
eligibility: College graduates or professionals with an interest in microfinance and international development, willing to relocate for the placement.
prepResources: Spanish, Portuguese, or French language skills significantly widen the range of eligible field placements.
location: Multiple countries in Latin America, Africa, and Asia

title: Ashoka Fellowship
description: A lifelong fellowship recognizing leading social entrepreneurs worldwide, with a particularly strong network across Latin America, providing a living stipend and global peer community.
eligibility: Social entrepreneurs with an already-operating, systems-changing initiative, nominated through Ashoka's search process.
prepResources: Ashoka fellows are typically identified through its own search network rather than open applications — building visibility for your initiative's impact is the practical path in.

title: Global Shapers Community
description: A global network of young leaders under 30 organized into city-based 'hubs' (including a large presence across Latin America) running local social-impact projects.
eligibility: Young people aged 18-30 with a track record of community engagement, applying to join an existing city hub.
prepResources: Check whether your city already has an active Global Shapers hub, since membership is hub-specific rather than centrally managed.

title: Fulbright Foreign Student Program (Latin America Commissions)
description: Fully funded graduate study scholarships in the United States, administered through individual Fulbright commissions in countries across Latin America.
eligibility: Citizens of a country with an active Fulbright commission, with a bachelor's degree and strong English proficiency.
prepResources: Each country's Fulbright commission runs its own specific timeline and priority fields — check your national commission's site directly.

title: Instituto Tecnológico y de Estudios Superiores de Monterrey (Tec de Monterrey) Scholarships
description: Merit- and need-based scholarships for undergraduate and graduate study at one of Latin America's leading universities, covering a significant portion of tuition costs.
eligibility: Prospective students (Mexican or international) admitted to a Tec de Monterrey program, meeting academic or need-based criteria.
prepResources: Scholarship applications are typically submitted alongside the general admissions process rather than separately.

title: Youth Ambassadors Program of the Americas
description: A three-week leadership exchange program in the United States for young leaders from countries across the Western Hemisphere, focused on civic engagement and community service.
eligibility: Secondary school students or recent graduates aged roughly 15-18, from participating countries in the Americas.
prepResources: Applications typically route through the local U.S. Embassy's exchange programs office in your country.

title: Australia Awards Scholarships
org: Australian Government (Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade)
description: Fully funded scholarships for students from partner countries across Asia, Africa, and the Pacific to pursue undergraduate or postgraduate study at Australian universities.
eligibility: Citizens of an Australia Awards partner country, meeting the specific academic and development-relevance criteria for their country.
prepResources: Eligible fields of study and partner countries are set annually — check your country's specific Australia Awards page before applying.

title: New Colombo Plan Scholarship
org: Australian Government (Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade)
description: A scholarship and mobility program funding Australian undergraduates to study and undertake internships across the Indo-Pacific region, building regional ties and language skills.
eligibility: Australian citizens who are current undergraduate students, applying through their home university's international office.
prepResources: Applications are coordinated through your Australian university's New Colombo Plan coordinator rather than directly with the government.

title: Fulbright New Zealand Graduate Awards
description: Fully funded scholarships for New Zealand graduates to pursue postgraduate study in the United States, and for American graduates to study or research in New Zealand.
eligibility: New Zealand or US citizens with a bachelor's degree, applying for reciprocal graduate study between the two countries.
prepResources: Fulbright New Zealand runs a distinct application process from the general Fulbright Program — use its own dedicated site and deadlines.

title: Study in New Zealand Excellence Awards
description: Merit-based scholarships supporting international students to pursue undergraduate or postgraduate study at New Zealand universities.
eligibility: International students admitted to a participating New Zealand institution, meeting academic merit criteria.
prepResources: Each participating New Zealand university manages its own scholarship stream, so check the specific institution's international scholarships page.

title: CSIRO Vacation Scholarships
org: Commonwealth Scientific and Industrial Research Organisation (CSIRO)
description: A paid summer research placement for undergraduate students at Australia's national science agency, working alongside active researchers on a real project.
eligibility: Undergraduate students, primarily in Australia, in their penultimate or final year of a science or engineering degree.
prepResources: CSIRO lists specific available projects by research division each cycle — matching your coursework to a specific project strengthens the application.

title: Erasmus Mundus Joint Master Degrees
description: Fully funded joint master's degree programs delivered across multiple European universities, covering tuition, travel, and living costs for students from any country worldwide.
eligibility: Bachelor's degree holders from any country worldwide, applying directly to a specific joint master's consortium program.
prepResources: Each joint program has its own separate application portal and deadline — browse the full program catalogue to find a fit for your field.

title: DAAD Scholarships for Development-Related Postgraduate Courses
description: Fully funded postgraduate scholarships for students from developing countries to pursue development-focused master's or PhD study in Germany.
eligibility: Citizens of a developing country with a bachelor's degree and, typically, professional work experience relevant to development.
prepResources: DAAD's own database lists the specific eligible master's programs by field — the scholarship application is tied to a specific program choice.

title: Marshall Scholarship
description: A prestigious scholarship funding American students to pursue graduate study at any UK university, intended to build lifelong understanding between the two countries.
eligibility: US citizens with a bachelor's degree (or graduating senior), with a strong academic record and demonstrated leadership.
prepResources: Applications require an institutional endorsement from your undergraduate university — starting the process early with your school's fellowships office is essential.

title: Swiss Government Excellence Scholarships
description: Scholarships for foreign postgraduate researchers and artists to pursue research or further study at Swiss universities, covering living costs and tuition.
eligibility: Foreign researchers with a master's degree or higher, applying through their home country's Swiss embassy.
prepResources: Applications are submitted through Swiss diplomatic missions abroad, not directly to Swiss universities — start with your home country's Swiss embassy.

title: Holland Scholarship
org: Dutch Ministry of Education, Culture and Science
description: A scholarship for non-EU/EEA students pursuing a full bachelor's or master's degree at a participating Dutch university, offered as a one-time award toward tuition.
eligibility: Non-EU/EEA students admitted to a full degree program at a participating Dutch institution.
prepResources: Applications are usually submitted through the specific Dutch university's international office alongside the admissions process.

title: Eiffel Excellence Scholarship Program
org: French Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs / Campus France
description: A scholarship for outstanding international students to pursue master's or PhD study at French institutions, covering a monthly stipend, travel, and health insurance.
eligibility: International students (non-French) under 30 (master's) or 35 (PhD), nominated by a French institution rather than applying directly.
prepResources: You must first apply to a French institution, which then nominates you for Eiffel — this isn't a direct-to-scholarship application.

title: Swedish Institute Scholarships for Global Professionals
description: A scholarship for professionals from eligible countries to pursue a master's degree in Sweden, combined with a leadership development program alongside coursework.
eligibility: Citizens of an eligible country (mostly outside the EU/EEA) with work experience and admission to a Swedish master's program.
prepResources: The Institute's own leadership development track runs alongside academic study — reviewing this component helps frame the application's motivation essay.

title: European Solidarity Corps Volunteering Projects
description: A program funding young people to volunteer on community and social projects across Europe, covering travel, accommodation, and a small living allowance.
eligibility: Young people aged 18-30 (with a broader participant band for organizers), registered on the European Solidarity Corps portal.
prepResources: Register on the Corps portal first, then browse and apply to specific projects posted by host organizations across Europe.

title: Fundação Estudar Leadership Scholarship
description: A leadership development scholarship and mentoring program for outstanding young Brazilians, combining a rigorous selection process with ongoing access to a national leadership network.
eligibility: Brazilian students or recent graduates with strong academic records and demonstrated leadership potential.
prepResources: The selection process includes case-based assessments similar to consulting interviews — practicing structured problem-solving is useful preparation.

title: 100,000 Strong in the Americas Innovation Fund
org: Partners of the Americas / U.S. Department of State
description: A grant program funding partnerships between universities in the US and Latin America to expand student exchange and study-abroad opportunities across the hemisphere.
eligibility: Higher education institutions in the US or Latin America proposing a joint student-mobility project; individual students apply through their university's program.
prepResources: Check whether your university already has an active 100,000 Strong partnership, since most student opportunities flow through an existing institutional grant.

title: Fulbright Commission for Educational Exchange in Brazil
description: Fully funded scholarships for Brazilian graduates to pursue master's or doctoral study in the United States, and for American scholars to research or teach in Brazil.
eligibility: Brazilian citizens with a bachelor's degree and strong English proficiency, or American scholars proposing a research/teaching project in Brazil.
prepResources: Fulbright Brazil runs its own specific application cycle and priority fields separate from other national commissions — use its dedicated site.

title: Instituto Ayrton Senna Educational Scholarship
description: A scholarship and mentoring initiative supporting Brazilian students from public schools to complete secondary education and pursue higher education pathways.
eligibility: Brazilian public school students demonstrating academic promise and financial need.
prepResources: The Institute partners directly with public school networks — check with your school's guidance office about current partnership programs.

title: Becas Chile Postgraduate Scholarship Program
org: National Agency for Research and Development (ANID), Chile
description: A government scholarship funding Chilean citizens to pursue master's or doctoral study at top universities abroad, covering tuition, travel, and living costs.
eligibility: Chilean citizens with an undergraduate or master's degree, admitted to a qualifying postgraduate program abroad.
prepResources: Secure admission to a specific overseas university program first, since the scholarship application requires proof of acceptance.

title: Colfuturo Scholarship-Loan Program
org: Fundación para el Futuro de Colombia (Colfuturo)
description: A scholarship-loan program funding Colombian graduates to pursue master's or doctoral study abroad, with partial loan forgiveness for returning to work in Colombia.
eligibility: Colombian citizens with an undergraduate degree, admitted to a qualifying postgraduate program outside Colombia.
prepResources: Colfuturo evaluates both academic merit and the applicant's plan to return and contribute professionally in Colombia.

title: Beca Presidente de la República (Peru)
org: National Scholarship and Educational Credit Program (PRONABEC), Peru
description: A government scholarship funding Peruvian citizens to pursue postgraduate study at top-ranked universities abroad, covering tuition, travel, and living costs.
eligibility: Peruvian citizens with an undergraduate degree, meeting PRONABEC's academic and university-ranking requirements.
prepResources: PRONABEC publishes an approved list of eligible universities and programs — confirm your target program appears on the current list.

title: Endeavor Entrepreneur Network (Latin America)
description: A global network selecting high-impact entrepreneurs for mentorship, access to capital, and a peer community, with one of its strongest chapter networks across Latin America.
eligibility: Founders of high-growth companies with real revenue and job creation, nominated or self-applying through a local Endeavor chapter.
prepResources: Endeavor selection panels evaluate real business metrics — have concrete revenue, growth, and team-size data ready.

title: New Zealand Development Scholarships
org: New Zealand Ministry of Foreign Affairs and Trade
description: Scholarships funding students from Pacific Island nations and other partner countries to pursue undergraduate or postgraduate study in New Zealand, tied to development priorities.
eligibility: Citizens of an eligible Pacific or partner country, admitted to a qualifying New Zealand program.
prepResources: Eligible countries and priority study fields are set annually by MFAT — check the current list before applying.

title: Pacific Leadership and Governance Precinct Fellowship
description: A fellowship supporting emerging leaders from Pacific Island nations to build governance and public policy skills through structured coursework and mentorship.
eligibility: Citizens of Pacific Island nations with a bachelor's degree and interest in public governance or policy work.
prepResources: Prior experience in a public-sector or community-governance role strengthens the application meaningfully.

title: Roger Conant Grants in Herpetology
org: Society for the Study of Amphibians and Reptiles (SSAR)
description: Annual grants for herpetological field research, education, and conservation across seven categories.
eligibility: Primarily SSAR student members; non-member students eligible under the international category.
prepResources: Choose the specific category (there are seven) that best matches your project's focus before applying.

title: Wilson Ornithological Society Research Grant
description: Four award programs funding avian research worldwide, from behavioral ecology to conservation.
eligibility: Ornithologists, graduate students, young professionals, and independent researchers without major funding access.
prepResources: This society funds independent and amateur ornithologists alongside academics — a clear, well-designed study matters more than institutional affiliation.

title: Frank M. Chapman Memorial Fund Grant
description: Small short-term awards for graduate research on avian evolutionary biology, including specimen-based and expeditionary bird research.
eligibility: Graduate students conducting avian evolutionary biology research.
prepResources: AMNH's own specimen collections are a resource worth referencing if your research involves museum-based avian study.

title: Society for Marine Mammalogy Small Grants in Aid of Research
description: Funds direct field and research costs for marine mammal science projects worldwide, with preference to first-time recipients and early-career researchers.
eligibility: Preference to first-time recipients and early-career researchers, including students and those within five years of a postdoc.
prepResources: This grant explicitly favors first-time applicants over previously-funded researchers — a clean, well-justified small budget helps.

title: Save Our Seas Foundation Small Grant
description: Funds short twelve- to eighteen-month projects on sharks, rays, skates, sawfishes, and chimaeras.
eligibility: Early-career scientists within five years of their most recent degree; maximum two small grants per career.
prepResources: A tightly scoped project on elasmobranchs (sharks, rays, and relatives) specifically, not general marine biology, fits this grant's focus.

title: Bat Conservation International Student Research Scholarship
description: Funds master's and PhD bat research addressing human-caused environmental change, human-bat conflict, or the ecology of vulnerable bat species.
eligibility: Registered graduate students (MSc/PhD) in good standing, with an advisor recommendation letter.
prepResources: A named faculty advisor's recommendation is a required part of this application — arrange this well before the deadline.

title: Primate Action Fund
description: Catalytic small grants for the conservation of endangered nonhuman primates and their habitats across Africa, Asia, and Latin America.
eligibility: Conservation practitioners and researchers, with priority to community-based and range-country work.
prepResources: Priority goes to researchers based in the primate's actual range country, not international teams working remotely.

title: IDEA WILD Equipment Grant
description: Provides field equipment such as cameras, GPS units, mist nets, and binoculars rather than cash, for biodiversity conservation research and education projects.
eligibility: Any individual conducting biodiversity conservation work, with priority to the Global South and under-resourced countries.
prepResources: Request specific equipment items rather than cash, since this grant is delivered as in-kind equipment support.

title: African Bird Club Conservation Award
description: Funds surveys, research, education projects, and expeditions on African birds and habitats, running since 1996.
eligibility: Individuals resident in African countries, or institutions based there; expeditions require local NGO participation.
prepResources: A partnership with a local African conservation NGO is required for the Expedition Award tier specifically.

title: Center for Plant Conservation Catherine H. Beattie Fellowship
description: An annual fellowship for the study and conservation of rare and endangered US plant species, especially in the Carolinas and southeastern US.
eligibility: Students, with preference to those studying US native rare plant species.
prepResources: A specific rare or endangered plant species (ideally in the southeastern US) grounds this fellowship's evaluation criteria.

title: Panthera Small Cat Action Fund
description: Supports in-situ conservation and research on the world's 31 small wild cat species, prioritizing field activities, equipment, and local salaries.
eligibility: Researchers and conservationists working on small wild cat species.
prepResources: This fund explicitly prioritizes small cats over the more commonly-funded big cats — proposals on lesser-known species have a real advantage.

title: Panthera Winston Cobb Memorial Fellowship
description: A field-based internship providing training and experience on a wild cat conservation project.
eligibility: Exceptional early-career conservationist.
prepResources: This fellowship is training-focused rather than independent-research funding — frame your application around building field skills, not running your own study.

title: State of the World's Sea Turtles (SWOT) Small Grant
description: Annual small grants for sea turtle research and conservation across networking, conservation-gap-filling, and behavior-change program areas.
eligibility: Sea turtle researchers and conservationists worldwide.
prepResources: Choose the specific program area (networking, gap-filling, or behavior-change) that best matches your project's aim.

title: Blue Corridors for Turtles Small Grants Fund
description: Funds community-engaged marine turtle research on migration, nesting and foraging habitat, and genetic connectivity.
eligibility: Researchers and community groups worldwide working on marine turtle migration and habitat connectivity.
prepResources: Community engagement is an explicit evaluation criterion — a project involving local stakeholders, not purely academic fieldwork, fits best.

title: Neotropical Grassland Conservancy Student Grant
description: Supports graduate students conducting conservation-focused field research in Latin American grassland ecosystems.
eligibility: Latin American students enrolled in a master's or PhD program at a US or South American university.
prepResources: A specific grassland ecosystem in Latin America (not general tropical forest research) is the required focus of this grant.

title: North American Native Fishes Association Conservation Research Grant
description: Funds research on the conservation of North America's native freshwater fish, especially threatened and endangered species, running since 2001.
eligibility: Open application, research-focused on native North American freshwater fish.
prepResources: A focus on threatened or endangered species specifically, rather than common native fish, aligns best with this grant's conservation mandate.

title: Society for Freshwater Science Graduate Student Conservation Research Award
description: Funds graduate research proposals addressing freshwater conservation topics.
eligibility: Graduate students only.
prepResources: A clear conservation-relevant angle (not just basic freshwater ecology) strengthens applications to this specific award.

title: ASAP Rapid Action Fund
org: IUCN SSC Asian Species Action Partnership (ASAP) / Fondation Segré
description: Urgent small grants for critically endangered Southeast Asian land and freshwater vertebrate species conservation action.
eligibility: Conservation practitioners and researchers working on ASAP-listed species.
prepResources: Confirm your target species is on the current ASAP list, since eligibility is restricted to those specifically listed species.

title: Wildlife Conservation Trust BEES Grants Programme
description: Funds Indian wildlife research and grassroots conservation on biodiversity, ecosystems, and endangered species, for projects up to ten months.
eligibility: Indian citizens with at least a graduate degree from a recognized university.
prepResources: A focused, ten-month-or-shorter field project on a specific Indian species or ecosystem fits this grant's scope.

title: British Ornithologists' Union Small Ornithological Research Grant
description: Small grants for any aspect of ornithological research worldwide, open to both amateur and professional researchers.
eligibility: BOU members; non-members from low- or middle-income countries are exempt from the membership requirement.
prepResources: Applicants from low- and middle-income countries can apply without a paid BOU membership — check the current exemption list.

title: Nagao Wetland Fund
org: Nagao Natural Environment Foundation / Ramsar Convention Secretariat
description: Supports small-scale wetland conservation research and action projects advancing Ramsar Strategic Plan goals across Asia and Oceania.
eligibility: Researchers and practitioners from eligible Asia-Pacific countries including Bangladesh, Cambodia, Indonesia, Philippines, and Vietnam.
prepResources: Tie your proposal explicitly to a Ramsar Strategic Plan goal, since alignment with that framework is a specific evaluation criterion.

title: Raptor Research Foundation Stephen R. Tully Memorial Grant
description: Supports research and conservation of raptors — hawks, eagles, falcons, owls, and vultures — targeted at those with limited access to alternative funding.
eligibility: Students and amateur researchers especially; RRF membership not required.
prepResources: This grant explicitly favors applicants without other major funding sources — highlight that lack of alternative support in your application.

title: Hawthornden Castle Fellowship
description: A month-long, fully-hosted writing retreat for fiction, poetry, nonfiction, and translation writers in a castle near Edinburgh, with no fee and no public readings or teaching required.
eligibility: Writers of any genre worldwide; no application fee.
prepResources: A concrete writing project description and sample work are the core of the application — the retreat gives uninterrupted time, not workshopping.

title: Fondation Jan Michalski Writing Residency
description: A two-week to three-month residency for writers and translators in purpose-built cabins in the Swiss Jura foothills, open to beginners and established writers alike.
eligibility: Open to all nationalities and ages; one application per person per year.
prepResources: The foundation reviews one application per writer per cycle — a clear, specific project proposal matters more than a long publication history.

title: Château de Lavigny Writer's Residence
description: A four-week residency (May-October) for published writers, translators, playwrights, and scriptwriters in a medieval chateau in the Swiss countryside.
eligibility: Must have at least one traditionally published book.
prepResources: Have your publication history ready to document, since this residency is restricted to already-published writers.

title: PEN/Heim Translation Fund Grant
description: Annual grants supporting the completion of literary translations into English, with preference given to early-career translators and underrepresented languages and authors.
eligibility: Translators working on a project bringing literature into English; roughly 10 projects funded per cycle.
prepResources: A sample translation excerpt alongside a clear rationale for why the source work deserves an English edition strengthens the application.

title: NEA Translation Project Fellowships
description: Federal fellowships for individual literary translators bringing prose, poetry, or drama into English, prioritizing underrepresented languages and voices.
eligibility: US-based individual translators with a specific translation project.
prepResources: NEA fellowships fund a specific named project, not general career support — a scoped, well-defined translation proposal is essential.

title: Akademie Schloss Solitude Fellowship
description: A transdisciplinary international residency in Stuttgart for artists, writers, composers, and scientists, with stays ranging from three to twelve months.
eligibility: International, cross-disciplinary applicants across art, writing, music, and science.
prepResources: Solitude values genuinely cross-disciplinary projects — framing your work in dialogue with another field strengthens the application.

title: Rijksakademie Residency
description: A prestigious one- to two-year studio residency in Amsterdam for visual artists of any discipline and nationality, with a monthly stipend and dedicated studio space.
eligibility: At least two to three years of independent professional practice as a visual artist.
prepResources: A strong, cohesive portfolio demonstrating an independent practice beyond art school matters more than institutional pedigree.

title: Harpo Foundation Grants for Visual Artists
description: Direct cash grants supporting under-recognized visual artists working across any medium, intended to fund materials, space, or time to make new work.
eligibility: US citizens or permanent residents aged 21 and older, not currently enrolled in a fine arts degree program.
prepResources: The Foundation specifically favors artists without a strong existing gallery or institutional presence — an emerging, under-recognized practice fits the mandate.

title: Civitella Ranieri Fellowship
description: A fully-funded six-week fellowship for writers, composers, and visual artists in a 15th-century Italian castle, with fellows selected by nomination only.
eligibility: Writers, composers, and visual artists nominated by the Foundation's network; no direct or self-applications accepted.
prepResources: Since this is nomination-only, building visibility through prior publications, exhibitions, or performances is the practical path toward being nominated.

title: Sacatar Fellowship
description: A fully-funded two-month residency for artists of all disciplines on Itaparica Island in Brazil, covering housing, studio space, meals, and laundry.
eligibility: Open call for artists of any age, discipline, or nationality; artists cover their own airfare.
prepResources: A clear description of what you plan to make or explore during the residency, rather than a general artist statement, strengthens the application.

title: ARCUS Project Artist-in-Residence
description: A long-running international contemporary art residency in rural Japan, running annually since 1994 and open to visual artists worldwide through an open call.
eligibility: International open call for visual artists of any nationality.
prepResources: ARCUS has a three-decade archive of past resident artists' projects, useful for understanding the program's curatorial focus.

title: Tokyo Arts and Space International Creators Program
description: A three-month fully-funded residency in Tokyo for international creators across disciplines, covering round-trip airfare, accommodation, and living support.
eligibility: International open call, annual application window typically in May-June.
prepResources: TOKAS runs a distinct annual open call each cycle — check the current year's specific theme or focus before applying.

title: Copland House Residency Award
description: A three- to eight-week residency for American composers at Aaron Copland's historic home in New York, providing free lodging, meals, and local transport.
eligibility: US citizens or permanent residents who are professional composers of any genre.
prepResources: A specific, in-progress composition project (not a general portfolio) is the basis of the application, due annually by June 1.

title: Copland House CULTIVATE Fellowship
description: A week-long, fully-scholarshipped intensive workshop and mentoring institute for emerging composers in their mid-to-late 20s.
eligibility: US citizens or permanent residents who are early-career composers, applying by the December deadline.
prepResources: Submit your strongest recent composition sample — CULTIVATE selects a small cohort for intensive mentorship, so quality over quantity of work matters.

title: Sangam House Writers' Residency
description: India's only funded international writers' residency, hosting published writers across all languages and disciplines for a fully funded stay.
eligibility: Published writers not yet at major commercial success, open to applicants worldwide.
prepResources: A clear, in-progress manuscript excerpt is more useful to the selection panel than a general writing history.
