title: Nordic Culture Point Mobility Funding
description: Travel and stay funding for professional artists and cultural practitioners moving within the Nordic and Baltic countries for work-related stays of up to two weeks.
eligibility: Must be a documented working professional in art or culture based in a Nordic or Baltic country.
prepResources: A clear professional purpose for the trip (residency, meeting, performance) is required, not general travel.

title: Translation House Looren Translation Grants
description: Grants tied to a one-month residency at Looren or Château de Lavigny in Switzerland, supporting professional literary translators, with a separate track for first-time translators.
eligibility: Published literary translators with a valid publishing contract, or first-time translators under a separate emerging track.
prepResources: A signed publishing contract for the work you intend to translate during the residency is required for the main track.

title: Bogliasco Fellowship
description: A one-month residential fellowship for arts and humanities projects — literature, music, film, visual arts, and more — in a seaside village near Genoa.
eligibility: Open to any age or nationality with a substantive project; not open to students still completing a degree.
prepResources: A specific, well-articulated project (not a general career-development goal) is central to the application.

title: Fine Arts Work Center Fellowship
description: A seven-month fellowship (October-May) for emerging writers and visual artists, providing an apartment, studio space, and a monthly stipend.
eligibility: Emerging writers (fiction, poetry) and visual artists selected through a juried process.
prepResources: A strong, focused writing or art sample matters more than a long resume — this program is explicitly built for early-career artists.

title: Art Omi Writers Translation Lab
description: A twelve-day intensive residency pairing writers with their translators for in-person collaborative work, with access to New York publishing contacts.
eligibility: International writers and translators applying as a pair.
prepResources: Apply together with your translator (or writer) counterpart — this residency is specifically structured around the writer-translator pair.

title: Jerome Hill Artist Fellowship
description: A flexible three-year fellowship for early-career generative artists across seven fields — dance, film, literature, music, theater, technology arts, and visual arts.
eligibility: Must reside in Minnesota or New York City's five boroughs, with two to ten years of professional experience.
prepResources: The three-year, unrestricted structure of this fellowship rewards artists with a clear multi-year creative direction, not a single project pitch.

title: The Africa Institute Creative Writers-in-Residence Fellowship
description: A residency and grant supporting African and diaspora creative writers working on a major literary project, based in Sharjah.
eligibility: Writers with at least one published work of African or African diaspora origin.
prepResources: A clearly scoped, in-progress major literary project (not a debut concept) is what this fellowship is designed to support.

title: EIAF Artist-in-Residence (Edeiken International Arts Foundation)
description: A fully-funded residency in Yokohama for professional musicians and sound artists, covering airfare, accommodation, living expenses, and materials.
eligibility: Musicians and sound artists aged 25 and older, living and working outside Japan, with five or more years of professional experience.
prepResources: A recorded portfolio demonstrating professional performance or composition history is central to the application.

title: Sleep Research Society Foundation Small Research Grant
description: Seed funding for pilot sleep-research studies by trainees and early-career investigators who lack institutional startup resources.
eligibility: SRS or AASM member; registered undergraduate/graduate student, or within 10 years of a terminal degree at assistant-professor level or earlier.
prepResources: This grant explicitly does not cover salary or travel — a lean, pilot-scale study design fits the funding model best.

title: National MPS Society Research Fellow Grant
description: Funds investigator-initiated research on mucopolysaccharidosis and mucolipidosis disorders, including a dedicated track for trainees.
eligibility: Postdoctoral fellows, clinical fellows, PhD candidates, or MD students studying MPS/ML disorders, open worldwide.
prepResources: A research plan specifically tied to MPS/ML disease mechanisms or therapy is required, not general rare-disease research.

title: Orphan Disease Center CDKL5 Pilot Grant Program
org: Orphan Disease Center, University of Pennsylvania / Loulou Foundation
description: One-year pilot grants funding basic and translational research on CDKL5 Deficiency Disorder, a rare neurodevelopmental condition, now in its tenth year.
eligibility: Open to researchers worldwide via a letter-of-intent process.
prepResources: A tightly scoped one-year pilot study with clear translational potential is favored over broad exploratory proposals.

title: Angelman Syndrome Foundation Innovation Seed Grants
description: Funds research on Angelman syndrome, including studies of the UBE3A gene mechanism and symptomatic therapies, through an open annual call for proposals.
eligibility: Open to researchers worldwide studying Angelman syndrome.
prepResources: Seed grants favor novel, higher-risk ideas over incremental extensions of existing funded work.

title: Angelman Syndrome Alliance Scientific Research Grant
description: Funds basic science research into Angelman syndrome, open to research teams across Europe and worldwide.
eligibility: Research teams studying Angelman syndrome, based in Europe or elsewhere worldwide.
prepResources: Coordinate with an established Angelman research group where possible, since collaborative proposals tend to be favored.

title: Alkermes Pathways Research Award
description: Supports junior-investigator research into schizophrenia, bipolar I disorder, and associated sleep and circadian disturbances, now in its eighth year.
eligibility: MD/PhD or equivalent within five years of a first academic appointment, or a current postdoctoral fellow, with no prior major federal grant.
prepResources: A clear connection to sleep/circadian biology within psychiatric illness strengthens fit with this award's specific focus.

title: MQ Mental Health Foundation Fellows Award
description: Supports early-career mental health scientists to establish research independence, funding work across depression, psychosis, and related domains.
eligibility: Early-career researchers, typically within a few years of completing a PhD.
prepResources: A clear articulation of how the fellowship builds toward research independence (not just a single study) is central to MQ's evaluation.

title: Cystic Fibrosis Foundation Postdoctoral Research Fellowship
description: Funds recent MD, PhD, or MD/PhD graduates conducting basic cystic fibrosis research, with two application rounds each year.
eligibility: Recent MD, PhD, or MD/PhD graduates conducting CF-focused basic research.
prepResources: A named mentor with an established CF research lab is typically expected as part of the application.

title: Cystic Fibrosis Foundation Student Traineeship Award
description: Supports undergraduate and graduate students conducting cystic fibrosis-related research training under a faculty mentor.
eligibility: Undergraduate or graduate students pursuing CF research under a named mentor.
prepResources: Identify a CF-focused faculty mentor first, since the traineeship structure requires named mentorship.

title: Thrasher Research Fund Early Career Award
description: Funds new investigators researching children's health topics to help them establish an independent research career, awarding up to 32 grants a year across two cycles.
eligibility: New or early-career researchers in child-health research with a qualified mentor; international applicants accepted.
prepResources: A clearly named mentor and a pediatric-health-focused research question are both required elements of the application.

title: ASN KidneyCure Pre-Doctoral Fellowship Award
description: Funds PhD students conducting original kidney biology or disease research under the guidance of a named sponsor.
eligibility: Current PhD students who have passed their qualifying exams, with no prior terminal doctoral degree.
prepResources: A sponsor with an established kidney-research lab and a clearly scoped dissertation-aligned project are both expected.

title: KidneyCure Ben J. Lipps Research Fellowship
description: Funds postdoctoral fellows conducting original nephrology research under sponsor guidance.
eligibility: Postdoctoral-level nephrology researchers with a named sponsoring lab.
prepResources: KidneyCure administers several linked fellowship tracks — check which specific one matches your career stage before applying.

title: EHA Junior Research Grant
description: Supports Europe-based early-career hematology researchers transitioning from trainee to independent investigator.
eligibility: Completed a PhD within the past four years, based in Europe.
prepResources: A research plan explicitly framed around establishing independence (not extending a supervisor's existing grant) fits this award's intent.

title: EHA Kick-off Grant
description: Funds high-risk, high-potential novel concepts in basic or translational hematology research proposed by early-career scientists.
eligibility: MD or PhD completed within the past eight years, based in Europe.
prepResources: This grant is explicitly meant for risky, early-stage ideas — a preliminary-data-heavy proposal is less competitive than a genuinely novel concept.

title: EHA Research Mobility Grant
description: Funds short research visits or mobility periods to another institution's research group for young hematology scientists, up to three months.
eligibility: Postdocs within one year of PhD graduation, or MDs in training within one year of hematology accreditation.
prepResources: Identify and confirm a specific host lab willing to receive you before applying, since the grant funds a defined visit, not open-ended travel.

title: ASH-EHA Translational Research Training in Hematology (TRTH)
org: American Society of Hematology & European Hematology Association
description: A year-long joint US-Europe training and mentoring program building translational hematology research careers for early-stage fellows.
eligibility: Early-stage hematology researchers and fellows.
prepResources: This is a structured mentorship program rather than a direct-funding grant — a clear statement of career goals in translational hematology matters most.

title: IASP Early Career Research Grant
org: International Association for the Study of Pain
description: Funds young researchers beginning independent pain-research careers worldwide.
eligibility: Early-career investigators without equal or greater prior grant support.
prepResources: A research plan focused specifically on pain mechanisms or treatment (not general neuroscience) fits this grant's scope best.

title: Foundation for Physical Therapy Research Grants
description: Funds physical therapy and rehabilitation research across specialty tracks including geriatric, pediatric, orthopedic, and cardiovascular/pulmonary care.
eligibility: US-licensed physical therapists or PT assistants who are APTA members.
prepResources: Several specialty tracks are reserved specifically for emerging investigators — check which track fits your career stage.

title: CIHR REDI Early Career Transition Award
description: Supports early-career researchers from underrepresented backgrounds transitioning to independent research careers in Canada, with one to three years of structured mentorship.
eligibility: Early-career researchers based in Canada from underrepresented groups.
prepResources: A clear articulation of how the researcher's background and research goals align with CIHR's equity, diversity, and inclusion priorities strengthens the application.

title: CDC-Hubert Global Health Award
description: Provides a stipend to past CDC Experience Program participants — medical and veterinary students, residents, physicians, and veterinarians — for continued global health field work.
eligibility: Must have completed a six-to-eight week CDC Experience Program rotation within the past five years.
prepResources: Prior completion of the CDC Experience Program is a strict prerequisite — this award is a continuation opportunity, not a first entry point.

title: Fulbright-Fogarty Fellowship in Public Health
description: Funds roughly ten researchers annually to conduct public health or clinical research in resource-limited settings abroad.
eligibility: US citizens with a relevant public health or clinical research background.
prepResources: A confirmed in-country research mentor and host institution are typically expected before final award, similar to standard Fulbright research grants.

title: Auckland Medical Research Foundation First Fellowship
description: Supports outstanding graduates roughly two years post-PhD pursuing a biomedical research career in New Zealand.
eligibility: Within approximately two years of completing a doctorate in a biomedical field.
prepResources: A clear plan for how this fellowship bridges toward an independent academic position strengthens the application.

title: AMRF Career Advancement Fellowship
description: Supports researchers three to five years post-PhD advancing toward independent biomedical research careers in New Zealand.
eligibility: Three to five years post-doctorate in a biomedical research field.
prepResources: Evidence of an emerging independent research direction (not just continuation of a supervisor's program) is important here.

title: Neurological Foundation of New Zealand Research Grants
description: Funds a range of neurological research training, from summer studentships through postdoctoral and clinical fellowships.
eligibility: New Zealand-affiliated researchers or students, with award level varying by career stage.
prepResources: Match your application to the correct funding tier (studentship, postdoctoral, or clinical fellowship) based on your current career stage.

title: Royal Society Newton International Fellowship
description: Funds early-career overseas researchers, including in biomedical and health sciences, to conduct two years of research in the United Kingdom.
eligibility: PhD awarded or thesis defended by the application deadline, with no more than five years of postdoctoral experience.
prepResources: A confirmed UK host institution and supervisor are required as part of the application.

title: British Academy International Fellowship
description: Supports emerging international research talent, including health-related humanities and social science work, to spend two years based in the United Kingdom.
eligibility: Early-career researchers globally, in humanities or social science fields relevant to health and society.
prepResources: A UK host institution must be secured before the fellowship can begin — identify potential hosts early in the process.

title: John Reinert Scholarship
description: Supports openly LGBTQ+ individuals training to become law enforcement officers or 911 dispatchers, awarded twice a year.
eligibility: Must be out as LGBTQ+ and enrolled in a US basic law enforcement, corrections, or dispatcher academy.
prepResources: A letter describing your career goals in law enforcement alongside your experience as an openly LGBTQ+ candidate is central to the application.

title: Ruby's Rainbow Scholarship
description: Funds continuing and higher education for adults with Down syndrome pursuing postsecondary, vocational, or continuing-education programs.
eligibility: Must have a diagnosis of Down syndrome and be enrolled in a postsecondary, vocational, or continuing-education program.
prepResources: Documentation of enrollment or acceptance into a specific program is required alongside the application.

title: Louise Tumarkin Zazove Foundation Scholarship
description: College scholarships for students with significant bilateral hearing loss, aimed at reducing financial barriers to higher education.
eligibility: Documented bilateral hearing loss, generally 50 decibels or greater unaided in both ears.
prepResources: Recent audiological documentation is required — request this from your audiologist well before the application window.

title: Point Foundation Access Scholarship
description: Supports first-generation and low-income LGBTQ+ and ally undergraduates with financial support and mentorship.
eligibility: At least half-time enrollment at an accredited nonprofit US college, first-generation or under-resourced background, minimum 2.0 GPA.
prepResources: Point Foundation evaluates leadership potential alongside financial need — community involvement or advocacy work strengthens the application.

title: Point Foundation Community College Scholarship
description: Supports LGBTQ+ and ally students earning an associate degree or transferring from community college to a four-year school.
eligibility: Enrolled in community college pursuing an associate's degree or a transfer pathway.
prepResources: A clear transfer plan or associate-degree timeline strengthens the application alongside financial documentation.

title: Roma Education Fund Tertiary Scholarship
description: Merit-based scholarships for Roma students pursuing bachelor's, master's, or doctoral degrees across Central and Eastern Europe.
eligibility: Must self-identify as Roma and be enrolled at an accredited university in one of the program's eligible countries.
prepResources: Eligible countries include Albania, Bosnia, Bulgaria, Croatia, Czechia, Hungary, Kosovo, North Macedonia, Moldova, Montenegro, Romania, Serbia, Slovakia, Turkey, and Ukraine — confirm your country's specific application process.

title: Roma Graduate Preparation Programme
org: Roma Education Fund / Open Society Foundations
description: A nine-month intensive preparatory program for Roma graduates aiming for English-language master's programs at top European universities.
eligibility: Roma applicants with a completed undergraduate degree in humanities or social sciences from a listed eligible country.
prepResources: Strong English proficiency and a clear target master's program are useful to identify before the preparatory program begins.

title: OWSD PhD Fellowship for Women Scientists
org: Organization for Women in Science for the Developing World (OWSD) / TWAS
description: Funds women from science-and-technology-lagging countries to pursue PhDs in natural, engineering, or IT sciences at Global South host institutes.
eligibility: Women scientists from designated science-and-technology-lagging countries who intend to return home after the fellowship.
prepResources: A clear plan to return to and contribute in your home country's scientific institutions is an explicit evaluation criterion.

title: OWSD Early Career Fellowship
org: Organization for Women in Science for the Developing World (OWSD)
description: Supports early-career women scientists from developing countries to advance their research programs.
eligibility: Women scientists in science-and-technology-lagging countries within a set number of years post-PhD.
prepResources: A concrete research plan tied to your home institution's capacity strengthens the application.

title: Zawadi Africa Education Fund Scholarship
description: Provides scholarships, leadership training, and life-skills programming for academically gifted, financially disadvantaged African girls.
eligibility: Female African nationals with demonstrated financial need and strong academic merit.
prepResources: The program combines scholarship funding with leadership training — prior community leadership experience is a plus.

title: IIE Odyssey Scholarship
description: A comprehensive scholarship covering tuition, housing, and living costs for refugee and displaced students pursuing higher education.
eligibility: Nominated refugee or displaced students through IIE's regional offices and partners in Latin America, Southeast Asia, and Sub-Saharan Africa.
prepResources: Nomination through an IIE regional partner is required — applicants generally cannot apply directly without that referral.

title: UNHCR DAFI Tertiary Refugee Scholarship Programme
description: Enables registered refugees to pursue undergraduate degrees in their country of asylum or upon return home, active in over 50 countries.
eligibility: Registered refugee or asylum seeker, generally under 28, with strong secondary school results.
prepResources: Applications are processed through local UNHCR offices in the country of asylum, not centrally.

title: AISES Scholarships (OASIS)
org: American Indian Science and Engineering Society
description: The largest scholarship provider to Indigenous STEM students in North America, matching students to multiple funded awards through a single application system.
eligibility: Enrolled member or descendant of a federal or state-recognized Tribe, Alaska Native Village, Native Hawaiian, Pacific Islander, or Indigenous Canadian community.
prepResources: A single OASIS application matches you to dozens of specific scholarship funds — complete your profile thoroughly to maximize matches.

title: Sertoma Scholarship for the Deaf or Hard of Hearing
description: Financial assistance for college-bound students with clinically significant bilateral hearing loss, awarded through local Sertoma clubs.
eligibility: Documented bilateral hearing loss, college-bound.
prepResources: Applications typically route through your local Sertoma club rather than a national portal — find your nearest chapter first.

title: Help America Hear Scholarship
description: Awards a hard-of-hearing high school senior a scholarship plus two hearing aids to support their transition to college.
eligibility: High school senior with documented hearing loss.
prepResources: Recent audiological records documenting hearing loss are required alongside the standard application.

title: Krishnan and Yegneswaran Family College Scholarship
description: A scholarship for college students who stutter, tied to the National Stuttering Association's mission and community involvement.
eligibility: Must stutter and be enrolled in or entering college.
prepResources: Involvement with NSA chapters or stuttering-community advocacy strengthens the application alongside academic records.

title: Change & Impact Scholarship
description: A full four-year scholarship covering tuition, room and board, and books, plus therapy and internship support for students who stutter.
eligibility: High school senior who stutters, with demonstrated financial need.
prepResources: This is a comprehensive, multi-year award — a strong essay on how stuttering has shaped your goals is central to selection.

title: DREAM.US Opportunity Scholarship
description: A large scholarship fund helping DACA, undocumented, and Temporary Protected Status students complete bachelor's degrees.
eligibility: Undocumented, DACA, or TPS status, enrolling at a specific partner college.
prepResources: Check the current list of Dream.US partner colleges, since the scholarship only applies to enrollment at those specific institutions.

title: Ascend Educational Fund Scholarship
description: Scholarships for first- and second-generation immigrant students, including undocumented students, graduating from New York City high schools.
eligibility: Immigrant or child of an immigrant, graduating from a New York City high school, with demonstrated financial need.
prepResources: A personal essay connecting your immigrant family background to your educational goals is a core part of the application.

title: Foster Care to Success Scholarship
description: Supports students who aged out of foster care or were orphaned by age 18 to pursue postsecondary study.
eligibility: Under 25, in foster care at the time of their 18th birthday or orphaned by 18, US resident.
prepResources: Documentation of foster care history is required alongside standard academic application materials.

title: Imagine H2O Pilot Fund
description: Provides recoverable grants and impact-linked loans helping water-technology startups pilot solutions with early adopters worldwide.
eligibility: Water-tech startups, generally under 12 years old, with under $5M revenue and under $20M equity raised.
prepResources: A confirmed pilot partner or early adopter willing to test the technology strengthens the application significantly.

title: Breakthrough Energy Fellows – Southeast Asia
description: A regional hub supporting early-stage climate-tech entrepreneurs developing technology capable of cutting emissions at scale.
eligibility: Scientists, engineers, and early-stage entrepreneurs with technology at an early technology-readiness level.
prepResources: A clear technical explanation of the emissions-reduction mechanism, not just a business model, is central to this fellowship's evaluation.

title: NESsT Latin America Accelerator Programme
description: A three-year tailored accelerator for social enterprises in Chile and Colombia providing capital and business support benefiting marginalized groups.
eligibility: Validation- or growth-stage enterprises in Chile or Colombia employing or benefiting low-income women, small producers, disabled people, or at-risk youth.
prepResources: Documented annual sales of at least $100,000 and a clear social-impact model targeting one of the priority groups are expected.

title: Alfanar Venture Philanthropy
description: The first venture philanthropy organization in the Arab world, providing multi-year grants plus a dedicated portfolio manager to growth-stage social enterprises.
eligibility: Growth-stage social enterprises in Egypt, Jordan, Lebanon, or Palestine focused on education, youth employment, women's empowerment, or environment.
prepResources: This is a multi-year, hands-on venture philanthropy relationship — being ready for close ongoing engagement with a portfolio manager matters as much as the pitch.

title: Cartier Women's Initiative Regional Award
description: A global award for women-led impact businesses, providing cash grants plus a leadership program across nine world regions.
eligibility: Business incorporated one to six years, revenue between $50,000 and $5 million, women hold the largest founder equity share.
prepResources: A clear articulation of both financial growth metrics and social/environmental impact strengthens the application.

title: AYuTe Africa Challenge
description: A pan-African competition identifying and funding young agritech entrepreneurs building solutions for smallholder farmers, run across multiple national competitions.
eligibility: Youth-led African agritech ventures based in one of the participating countries.
prepResources: Enter through your specific country's national AYuTe competition first, since regional funding flows from those national winners.
location: Ethiopia, Kenya, Nigeria, Rwanda, Senegal, Tanzania, Malawi, Uganda

title: EIT Food Empowering Women in Agrifood Programme
description: An EU-backed program building entrepreneurial capacity for women-led agrifood startups through mentoring and a demo day.
eligibility: Women-led agrifood entities registered in a Horizon Europe-eligible country; separate tracks for early-stage and growth-stage founders.
prepResources: Choose the correct track (Advance for established ventures, Grow for early-stage founders) based on your company's age and technology readiness.

title: Innovations in Mental Health Fund
description: A free, highly selective twelve-week program supporting startups tackling depression, anxiety, and psychosis with mentorship and investment access.
eligibility: Companies working on mental health solutions, open to any country.
prepResources: A working product addressing a specific mental health condition, not a general wellness app, fits this program's focus.

title: UNHCR Refugee-led Innovation Fund
description: Provides direct funding, mentoring, and expertise to organizations led by forcibly displaced and stateless people building community-based innovations.
eligibility: Organizations where at least half of leadership have lived experience of displacement or statelessness.
prepResources: A clear, community-rooted innovation with existing leadership by displaced people (not just serving displaced people) is central to eligibility.

title: Grand Challenges Canada Stars in Global Health
description: A seed funding stream for bold early-stage global health innovations, part of a broader seed-to-scale funding model.
eligibility: Social enterprises, nonprofits, for-profits, or research institutions legally incorporated in eligible low- or middle-income countries or Canada.
prepResources: A bold, integrated idea (combining a science, technology, or business innovation) is explicitly favored over incremental improvements.

title: Echoing Green Fellowship
description: An eighteen-month fellowship for early-stage social entrepreneurs, providing a stipend, capacity-building support, and a peer community.
eligibility: Original founder or founding team member able to work full-time on the venture, proficient in English.
prepResources: Echoing Green looks for founders deeply embedded in the problem they're solving — personal proximity to the issue strengthens the application.

title: Beyond the Grid Fund for Africa
description: A results-based financing facility funding off-grid energy access companies serving displacement-affected and underserved communities in Africa.
eligibility: Off-grid or mini-grid energy companies operating in eligible African countries.
prepResources: This is a results-based facility — funding is tied to verified connections delivered, so proven delivery capacity matters more than concept alone.

title: Milken-Motsepe Prize in Circular Economy
description: A competition for scalable, tech-driven African businesses transforming waste into value through circular economy business models.
eligibility: African-based companies with scalable circular-economy or waste-to-value business models.
prepResources: Demonstrated waste-diversion or value-recovery metrics from an operating business strengthen the application over concept-only proposals.

title: Global LEAP Solar E-Waste Challenge
description: A challenge funding innovative e-waste management approaches within the off-grid solar sector in Sub-Saharan Africa.
eligibility: Companies with e-waste management solutions serving the off-grid solar sector in Sub-Saharan Africa.
prepResources: A clear e-waste collection or recycling model tied specifically to solar product lifecycle is the core evaluation criterion.

title: The Accessibility Fund
description: A multi-year cohort program funding small, disability-focused organizations, especially in the Global South, with unrestricted grants and a nonprofit accelerator track.
eligibility: Small disability-focused organizations, with priority given to those based in the Global South.
prepResources: Unrestricted funding is prioritized for organizations led by or deeply embedded with disabled communities themselves.

title: Microsoft AI for Accessibility Grant
description: A twelve-month grant and support program for researchers, startups, and nonprofits building assistive technology using AI.
eligibility: Startups, nonprofits, or researchers developing accessibility-focused technology.
prepResources: A working prototype demonstrating a specific accessibility use case (not a general AI idea) is favored in this program.

title: Women Who Tech FemTech + HealthTech Grant
description: A global grant challenge for women-led FemTech and HealthTech startups addressing healthcare access gaps.
eligibility: Women-led early-stage startups in FemTech or HealthTech, open internationally.
prepResources: A working product or strong prototype addressing a specific healthcare access gap strengthens the pitch.

title: UNICEF Venture Fund
description: An equity-free funding vehicle for open-source, frontier-tech startups improving child health, education, and wellbeing outcomes.
eligibility: For-profit early-stage startups registered in a UNICEF program (low- or middle-income) country, with an open-source requirement.
prepResources: Being prepared to open-source your core technology is a strict requirement — factor this into your IP strategy before applying.

title: Postcode Lotteries Green Challenge
description: An annual startup competition for green innovations tackling climate change, plastic pollution, or food waste.
eligibility: Entrepreneurs based in Germany, the UK, Netherlands, Norway, or Sweden with a market-ready product within two years.
prepResources: A clear path to market within two years (not a research-stage concept) is required, since this challenge favors near-commercial ventures.

title: AirAsia Foundation Grant Programme
description: A grant program funding ASEAN-based social enterprises and community groups with a proven track record of social or environmental impact.
eligibility: ASEAN-registered and led organizations with at least a two-year track record.
prepResources: Two years of documented impact data is a hard eligibility requirement — this is not a fund for brand-new organizations.

title: Villgro Incubation Program
description: India's longest-running social enterprise incubator, providing seed funding and incubation across health, agribusiness, climate action, and gender-inclusion sectors.
eligibility: Early-stage social enterprises based in India in health, agriculture, climate, or gender-inclusion focus areas.
prepResources: Villgro evaluates both the technical innovation and the founding team's commitment to full-time execution.

title: Segal Family Foundation Grant
description: A trust-based philanthropic funder giving first-time and renewal grants to grassroots, African-led organizations serving youth.
eligibility: African-led, grassroots NGOs or social enterprises operating in Sub-Saharan Africa.
prepResources: Segal emphasizes trust-based, unrestricted funding — a clear, honest account of organizational needs (not just program pitches) is welcomed.

title: Miller Center GSBI Accelerator
org: Miller Center for Social Entrepreneurship, Santa Clara University
description: A university-based social enterprise accelerator focused on climate resilience and women's economic empowerment, providing mentorship from a global business-leader network.
eligibility: Early- to growth-stage social enterprises worldwide, admitted through an application-based cohort process.
prepResources: Mentorship is the core value here rather than direct funding — being ready to actively engage with volunteer executive mentors matters most.

title: Y. Eva Tan Conservation Reporting Fellowship
description: A six-month remote fellowship supporting young or aspiring journalists from biodiversity hotspot countries reporting on environmental issues.
eligibility: Young or aspiring journalists from the world's biodiversity hotspot countries.
prepResources: A story pitch grounded in a specific local ecosystem or conservation issue, not a general environmental interest, strengthens the application.

title: Journalismfund Europe Environmental Journalism Grant
description: Supports cross-border investigative journalism projects on environmental issues across Europe.
eligibility: Professional journalists or teams working on a cross-border European environmental story.
prepResources: A genuinely cross-border angle (involving more than one European country) is a core requirement, not just an environmental topic.

title: Earth Journalism Network Biodiversity Story Grant
description: Story grants expanding the quality and quantity of biodiversity journalism coverage worldwide.
eligibility: Journalists in any medium, including early-career reporters, with stories publishing within the grant cycle.
prepResources: A confirmed publication outlet for the finished story is generally expected as part of the grant application.

title: Fund for Investigative Journalism Seed and Regular Grants
description: Small grants for preliminary and full investigative reporting projects across print, online, broadcast, books, documentaries, and podcasts.
eligibility: Freelance journalists, staff reporters, or media organizations, open internationally.
prepResources: Seed grants fund the preliminary research phase before a full story is locked in — a clear initial investigative lead is enough to apply.

title: Alicia Patterson Fellowship
description: Supports in-depth written journalism projects over six- to twelve-month periods.
eligibility: Professional journalists proposing a specific in-depth reporting project.
prepResources: A tightly scoped, single-topic project (not a general beat) matters more than years of experience for this fellowship.

title: Diversify Photo Eyewitness Photojournalism Grant
description: Supports projects by independent photojournalists from backgrounds historically underrepresented in global media.
eligibility: Independent photojournalists from underrepresented backgrounds.
prepResources: A concrete story with existing access to subjects and locations strengthens the proposal over a general thematic interest.

title: Inge Morath Award
description: An annual award to a woman or nonbinary photographer under 30 to complete a long-term documentary project.
eligibility: Woman or nonbinary photographer under 30 years old.
prepResources: A long-term project already underway (not a brand-new concept) with a portfolio of early images is expected.

title: Firelight Media Documentary Lab
description: A twelve-month fellowship for filmmakers of color working on a first or second feature documentary, with production mentorship.
eligibility: Filmmakers of color working on a first or second feature documentary at the post-production stage.
prepResources: A rough cut or substantial footage already shot is generally expected, since this lab supports films already in post-production.

title: AXS Film Fund
description: Supports documentary and nonfiction media creators with disabilities and from underserved communities.
eligibility: Individuals living with disabilities or creators from underserved communities working on nonfiction media projects.
prepResources: A clear articulation of the filmmaker's own lived experience connected to the project's subject matter strengthens the application.

title: Points North Fellowship
description: Supports early-to-mid-career nonfiction filmmakers, including an unrestricted grant for a feature documentary in post-production.
eligibility: Early- to mid-career filmmakers with nonfiction projects, generally in post-production.
prepResources: A rough cut and a clear distribution or festival strategy strengthen an application at this later production stage.

title: Carter Center Rosalynn Carter Fellowship for Mental Health Journalism
description: Supports journalists producing in-depth reporting on mental health issues, with structured mentoring throughout the fellowship.
eligibility: Professional journalists, with both domestic (US) and international fellowship cohorts.
prepResources: A specific mental-health reporting angle (not a general health beat) and a named mentor relationship are part of this fellowship's design.

title: Kari Howard Fund for Narrative Journalism
description: Funds the development of long-form narrative journalism pieces, honoring former Los Angeles Times editor Kari Howard.
eligibility: Print journalists or a leading multimedia team, with projects publishable in English anywhere.
prepResources: A strong narrative arc and a confirmed or likely publication outlet strengthen the application.

title: Elizabeth Neuffer Fellowship
description: Supports the career development of an early-stage female or nonbinary journalist covering human rights and social justice, including MIT coursework and major newsroom internships.
eligibility: Early-stage female or nonbinary journalist, international applicants eligible.
prepResources: A demonstrated commitment to human-rights reporting through prior published work is central to the selection process.

title: Howard G. Buffett Fund for Women Journalists
description: An eight-year annual grant fund supporting women and nonbinary journalists' reporting projects worldwide.
eligibility: Women or nonbinary full-time professional journalists with at least one year of experience.
prepResources: A specific, well-scoped reporting project with a clear timeline strengthens applications to this fund's annual cycle.

title: Fetisov Journalism Awards
description: An international award across four categories — peace, civil rights, investigative reporting, and environmental journalism.
eligibility: Professional journalists, journalism organizations, or freelancers; self-nomination is allowed.
prepResources: Choose the category (peace, civil rights, investigative, or environmental) that most precisely matches your published work.

title: One World Media Fellowship
description: A year-long program supporting global journalism and documentary storytelling from the Global South, with an assigned executive producer and workshops.
eligibility: Mid-career journalists or filmmakers with three or more years of experience reporting on Global South countries.
prepResources: A specific Global South story idea with a clear access plan (contacts, location knowledge) strengthens the application.

title: Sigma Awards
description: An international competition celebrating the best data journalism projects worldwide, spanning portfolio and individual-project categories.
eligibility: Open globally to data journalists and newsrooms.
prepResources: A clean, well-documented data methodology alongside the finished story is what judges evaluate most closely.

title: AIR AMPLIFY New Voices Program
description: A program for underrepresented audio producers to develop radio and podcast projects.
eligibility: Self-identified members of underrepresented groups, aged 18 and older, based in the US.
prepResources: A clear audio story concept with a sample of prior audio work (even informal) strengthens the application.

title: CanadaLand Audio Journalism Fellowship
description: A fellowship for producing a twenty- to thirty-minute audio journalism project published on the Canadaland network.
eligibility: Open application window for Canada-based audio journalists.
prepResources: A clearly scoped single-episode story (not a full series pitch) fits this fellowship's production timeline.

title: PowerLines Documentary Photography Fellowship
description: A three-month documentary photography fellowship focused on economic and human issues.
eligibility: At least three years of professional photography experience with a documentary or photojournalism background.
prepResources: A cohesive portfolio demonstrating sustained, empathetic documentary work (not single strong images) is central to selection.

title: McGraw Fellowship for Business Journalism
org: Craig Newmark Graduate School of Journalism, CUNY
description: Funding and editorial support for deeply reported business and investigative enterprise stories.
eligibility: Freelance or staff journalists with at least five years of professional experience.
prepResources: A business story with a clear accountability or investigative angle (not general business news) fits this fellowship's mandate.

title: G. Unger Vetlesen Prize
description: Sometimes called the Nobel Prize of Earth sciences, awarded roughly every two years for scientific achievement in understanding Earth, its history, or its relation to the universe.
eligibility: Open to any individual worldwide; awarded through nomination.
prepResources: A sustained record of foundational contributions to earth science, not a single paper, is what this prize recognizes.

title: BSA Graduate Student Research Award
description: Supports graduate student research proposals across botanical sciences, open to students worldwide, not limited to the US.
eligibility: BSA member, full-time graduate student, with a BSA-member faculty advisor.
prepResources: A clearly scoped botanical research project with a defined budget line-item plan fits this award's format well.

title: Torrey Botanical Society Graduate Student Research Fellowship
description: Funds graduate research in plant biology and botany.
eligibility: Graduate student conducting botanical research.
prepResources: A concise research summary tied to plant biology fundamentals (taxonomy, physiology, ecology) fits this fellowship's scope.

title: GSA Graduate Student Research Grant
description: Provides partial support for master's or doctoral thesis research in the geological sciences.
eligibility: GSA member enrolled in a North, Central American, or Mexican university earth-science graduate program.
prepResources: A tightly scoped thesis-research budget (field costs, lab analysis) is the core of this application, not a broad research statement.

title: Simons Dissertation Fellowship in Mathematics
description: Provides research support for graduate students in the final years of a mathematics PhD.
eligibility: PhD students in years four to five of a US mathematics doctoral program.
prepResources: A strong letter from your dissertation advisor describing progress toward a specific research problem is central to this fellowship.

title: Ludo Frevel Crystallography Scholarship
description: Supports promising graduate students pursuing crystallographically-oriented research.
eligibility: Graduate students with a crystallography-related research proposal, open internationally.
prepResources: A clear connection between your proposed research and X-ray or diffraction-based crystallography methods strengthens the application.

title: Mineralogical Society of America Grant for Research in Crystallography
description: Funds research in mineralogical crystallography, the Edward H. Kraus Fund.
eligibility: Graduate or undergraduate students, beginning faculty, and young researchers in mineralogical crystallography.
prepResources: A specific mineral system or crystallographic method (not general geology) is the focus this grant supports.

title: L'Oréal-UNESCO For Women in Science International Rising Talents
description: Selects fifteen top women scientists globally each year from national and regional L'Oréal-UNESCO winners for added international recognition and support.
eligibility: Women scientists at PhD or postdoctoral level who already hold a national or regional L'Oréal-UNESCO fellowship.
prepResources: Winning a national or regional L'Oréal-UNESCO award first is a prerequisite before being considered for this international tier.

title: Royal Astronomical Society Undergraduate Summer Bursary
description: Funds six- to ten-week undergraduate summer research projects in astronomy or geophysics.
eligibility: Undergraduate named by a Fellow of the Royal Astronomical Society supervisor.
prepResources: Identify a supervisor who is already a Fellow of the RAS, since the bursary requires their formal nomination.

title: Explorers Club Exploration Fund Grant
description: Funds fieldwork for graduate and postgraduate students and early-career field scientists across disciplines including botany, earth sciences, marine research, and paleontology.
eligibility: Graduate/postgraduate students or early-career scientists conducting fieldwork.
prepResources: A concrete field expedition plan with a defined location and budget matters more than a broad research question.

title: Explorers Club Rising Explorer Grant
description: Supports original scientific field research conducted by high schoolers and undergraduates.
eligibility: High school students, college undergraduates, or independent researchers at an equivalent level.
prepResources: This grant is explicitly aimed at younger researchers — a well-mentored but genuinely student-led project fits best.

title: Company of Biologists Travelling Fellowship
description: Funds collaborative lab visits for graduate students and postdocs in cell biology, developmental biology, and experimental biology.
eligibility: Graduate student or postdoc, no nationality restriction.
prepResources: A confirmed host lab willing to receive the visit is required before applying, since the fellowship funds a specific collaboration.

title: APS Scholarship for Minority Undergraduate Physics Majors
description: Supports underrepresented-minority undergraduates majoring in physics.
eligibility: Undergraduate physics major from an underrepresented minority group at a US institution.
prepResources: A clear statement connecting your physics coursework and career goals strengthens this scholarship application.

title: BES Small Research Grant
description: Funds innovative ecological science projects lasting up to twelve months.
eligibility: BES member; early-career defined as within five years of starting a research career or completing a PhD.
prepResources: A tightly scoped, twelve-month-or-shorter ecological project fits this grant's format better than an open-ended research program.

title: BES Early Career Project Grant
description: Larger project grants supporting outstanding early-career ecologists.
eligibility: BES member, early-career ecologist.
prepResources: This larger grant tier expects a more developed project plan and preliminary data compared to the Small Research Grant.

title: Genetics Society of America DeLill Nasser Award for Professional Development
description: Provides funding for early-career geneticists to attend a conference or training course.
eligibility: Early-career geneticists affiliated with GSA.
prepResources: A specific named conference or course with clear relevance to your genetics research strengthens the application.

title: ZSL EDGE Fellowship
description: A 28-month leadership and training program for conservationists focused on Evolutionarily Distinct and Globally Endangered species.
eligibility: Early-career conservationists with under 10 years of experience, resident or national of the project country in the Global South.
prepResources: A specific EDGE-listed species and country tied to your own nationality/residency is required for eligibility.

title: JSPS Postdoctoral Fellowship for Research in Japan
org: Japan Society for the Promotion of Science
description: Funds foreign postdoctoral researchers to conduct research at Japanese institutions under a host researcher.
eligibility: PhD holders of non-Japanese nationality with no permanent residency in Japan.
prepResources: A confirmed Japanese host researcher willing to accept you is required as part of the application.

title: FRQNT Doctoral Training Scholarship
org: Fonds de recherche du Québec – Nature et Technologies
description: Funds doctoral students in natural sciences, mathematics, and engineering in Québec.
eligibility: Canadian citizen or permanent resident, enrolled doctoral student with a valid Québec health card.
prepResources: Confirm enrollment or admission at a Québec institution first, since this scholarship is tied specifically to Québec universities.

title: Wenner-Gren Foundation Dissertation Fieldwork Grant
description: Funds doctoral dissertation fieldwork advancing anthropological knowledge, across any subfield or world region.
eligibility: Enrolled doctoral student (or international equivalent) who has met all requirements except the dissertation.
prepResources: A clearly scoped fieldwork plan with defined location, duration, and methodology is the core of this application.

title: Herchel Smith Postdoctoral Research Fellowship
org: Herchel Smith Fund, University of Cambridge
description: Supports early-career researchers in biological and physical sciences, including chemistry and mathematics, at Cambridge.
eligibility: Early-career researchers in a relevant science discipline.
prepResources: A clear alignment between your research area and an existing Cambridge research group strengthens the application.

title: Türkiye Bursları (Turkey Scholarships)
description: Turkey's flagship fully-funded scholarship covering associate, bachelor's, master's, and PhD study at Turkish universities for international students worldwide.
eligibility: Age under 21 (bachelor's), under 30 (master's), under 35 (PhD); minimum 70-90% academic average depending on level.
prepResources: Roughly 5,000 scholarships are awarded from over 165,000 applicants each year — a strong, specific field-of-study choice helps differentiation.

title: Stipendium Hungaricum
description: Hungary's flagship scholarship for international students at Hungarian universities, delivered through bilateral education agreements with over 100 partner countries.
eligibility: Must hold citizenship of a partner country with a bilateral agreement and be nominated by the home country's sending partner.
prepResources: Check with your home country's designated Sending Partner organization, since nomination (not direct application) determines eligibility.

title: Romanian Government Scholarship
description: Annual scholarships for non-EU citizens to study bachelor's, master's, or PhD programs at Romanian public universities.
eligibility: Minimum average of 7 out of 10 in prior schooling; excludes medicine, dental medicine, and pharmacy for bachelor's/master's tracks.
prepResources: A preparatory Romanian-language year is included for most non-Romanian-taught programs — factor this into your study timeline.

title: MEXT Scholarship (Embassy Recommendation)
description: Japan's government scholarship for foreign students, administered through Japanese embassies and consulates worldwide, covering undergraduate through graduate research study.
eligibility: Apply through your local Japanese embassy or consulate rather than directly to MEXT; open to countries with diplomatic relations with Japan.
prepResources: First-stage screening happens at the embassy level between May and August — check your local embassy's specific timeline.

title: Government of Brunei Darussalam Scholarship
description: Fully funded undergraduate and master's scholarships for international students at Brunei's public universities.
eligibility: Citizens of ASEAN, Commonwealth, or OIC member countries, aged 18-25 for undergraduate and under 35 for master's.
prepResources: This scholarship is only available for first-time study in Brunei — prior study there disqualifies an application.

title: Icelandic Government Scholarship (Icelandic as a Second Language)
description: Annual scholarships, roughly a dozen awarded, for foreign students to study a BA program in Icelandic as a Second Language at the University of Iceland.
eligibility: At least one year of prior university humanities study and basic existing Icelandic proficiency.
prepResources: Complete Icelandic Online levels 1 and 2 (a free online course) before applying, since basic proficiency is a stated prerequisite.

title: Israel Ministry of Foreign Affairs Scholarship
description: Scholarship for holders of a BA/BSc or higher to pursue an MA, PhD, postdoctoral, or special program at Israeli universities, plus a summer Hebrew program option.
eligibility: Must hold a BA/BSc or higher, be under 35 at the start of the academic year, and be a citizen of an eligible country.
prepResources: Check the current list of eligible citizenships, since this scholarship is restricted to a specific set of countries.

title: Thailand International Postgraduate Programme (TIPP)
description: A fully funded master's-level scholarship for students from developing countries, nominated by their government, as part of Thai development cooperation.
eligibility: Bachelor's degree required; must be officially nominated by the National Focal Point for International Development Cooperation.
prepResources: Nomination through your national development-cooperation agency is required — check with your government's relevant ministry first.

title: Stefan Banach NAWA Programme
description: A Polish development-cooperation scholarship funding master's-level study at Polish universities for citizens of 36 designated developing countries.
eligibility: Citizens of one of 36 eligible countries including Georgia, India, Indonesia, Kenya, and Vietnam.
prepResources: A preparatory language course is included — check the current list of 36 eligible countries before applying.

title: Vietnam Government Scholarship (VIED)
description: Government-funded scholarships for international students to pursue bachelor's, master's, and doctoral study at Vietnamese universities across multiple fields.
eligibility: Awarded via bilateral agreements and ministry channels; separate tracks exist for specific fields.
prepResources: Check whether your country has a current bilateral agreement with Vietnam's Ministry of Education and Training.

title: Slovak Republic Government Scholarship
description: Funds bachelor's, master's, and doctoral studies for students from Official Development Assistance-eligible countries, plus a separate mobility program.
eligibility: Citizenship of an ODA-eligible country for the development-cooperation track; roughly 70 scholarships allocated annually.
prepResources: The application window typically runs March 25 to May 25 — mark this specific window since it's narrower than many national scholarships.

title: Bulgarian Government Scholarship
description: Bilateral-agreement scholarships for international students to study at Bulgarian public higher education institutions, administered through Bulgarian embassies.
eligibility: Primarily processed through embassy channels; roughly 1,000 foreign students enrolled annually under the scheme.
prepResources: Contact your national Bulgarian embassy directly, since this scholarship is primarily embassy-channeled rather than centrally applied for.

title: Moroccan Government Scholarship (AMCI)
description: Fully funded scholarship for international students to pursue undergraduate, master's, and PhD study at Moroccan public universities and research institutions.
eligibility: Citizens of countries with a bilateral scholarship agreement with Morocco, applying through diplomatic channels; French or Arabic proficiency required.
prepResources: Apply through your country's diplomatic channel to Morocco, since this scholarship is not open for direct online application.

title: Swiss Government Excellence Scholarships (ESKAS)
description: Swiss government research scholarships for early-career foreign researchers holding a master's degree, plus a separate art-scholarship track, open to applicants from 183 countries.
eligibility: Completed master's degree required; must have a Swiss academic supervisor willing to direct the research.
prepResources: Secure a Swiss academic supervisor's agreement before applying, since this is a required part of the application package.

title: Egypt Government Scholarship
description: Fully funded scholarship for international students to pursue bachelor's, master's, and PhD degrees at Egyptian public universities.
eligibility: Age limits of 22 for undergraduate, 30 for master's, 35 for PhD; English or Arabic proficiency required.
prepResources: Applications open annually between March and June — prepare English or Arabic proficiency documentation ahead of that window.

title: Darmasiswa Scholarship
description: A non-degree, one-year scholarship for foreign nationals to study Indonesian language, arts, and culture at 64 host universities.
eligibility: Citizens of countries with diplomatic ties to Indonesia, aged 18-32, with English proficiency documentation.
prepResources: Choose from 64 participating host universities based on your specific arts, music, or language interest.

title: KNB Scholarship (Kemitraan Negara Berkembang)
description: A scholarship for students from developing countries to pursue bachelor's, master's, or doctoral degrees at participating Indonesian universities, having funded over 1,290 recipients from 94 countries since 2008.
eligibility: Citizens of a designated developing country, with degree-level eligibility varying by track.
prepResources: A year of free Indonesian language classes is included — factor this preparatory period into your overall study timeline.

title: Norwegian Quota Scheme
description: A scholarship and loan scheme for students from developing countries, Eastern Europe, and Central Asia to pursue master's, PhD, or professional degrees in Norway, with loan forgiveness upon returning home.
eligibility: Limited to DAC-list lower-middle-income countries, Central/Eastern Europe, Central Asia, and Palestinian students, via partnered institutions only.
prepResources: Only Norwegian institutions with an existing partnership agreement can host Quota Scheme students — confirm this before applying to a specific university.

title: IKY Scholarships for Modern Greek Language and Culture
description: Courses and seminars in Modern Greek Language and Culture for international students, lasting up to eight months.
eligibility: Open to foreign nationals interested in Greek language and culture study.
prepResources: This program is non-degree and culture/language-focused — a clear interest statement in Greek studies is sufficient without prior formal coursework.

title: TaiwanICDF Scholarship Program
org: Government of Taiwan (International Cooperation and Development Fund)
description: A fully financed scholarship for international students from partner and diplomatic-ally countries to pursue undergraduate, graduate, and doctoral programs at Taiwanese universities.
eligibility: Must meet the partner university's admission requirements and obtain a Taiwanese Alien Resident Certificate.
prepResources: Check which Taiwanese universities currently partner with ICDF, since the scholarship is tied to specific designated institutions.

title: Global Korea Scholarship (GKS/KGSP)
description: South Korea's flagship government scholarship inviting over 2,000 international students annually to pursue undergraduate and graduate degrees at Korean universities.
eligibility: Under 40 years old, applicant and parents must hold non-Korean citizenship; apply via Embassy Track or University Track.
prepResources: Choose between the Embassy Track (via your national Korean embassy) or University Track (direct to a specific university) based on which timeline suits you.

title: Chinese Government Scholarship (CSC)
description: China's national scholarship program funding over 51,000 international students annually at 289 designated Chinese universities across all academic levels.
eligibility: Admission requirements vary by university and program; applications generally open December through April.
prepResources: Choose a designated CSC partner university carefully, since scholarship terms and stipend levels vary significantly by institution.

title: Heydar Aliyev International Education Grant Program
description: Azerbaijan's presidential scholarship program funding roughly 100 international students annually across preparatory, undergraduate, graduate, doctoral, and medical residency programs.
eligibility: Citizens of OIC, Non-Aligned Movement, Small Island Developing States, or countries with historical ties to Azerbaijan, via a two-stage nomination process.
prepResources: This program uses a two-stage nomination followed by a formal application through SIACAS — start with the nomination stage well in advance.

title: World in Serbia Scholarship
description: A scholarship for students from Non-Aligned Movement member and observer countries across Africa, Asia, and Central/South America, currently supporting over 536 scholars from 127 countries.
eligibility: Applicant countries must be Non-Aligned Movement members or observers.
prepResources: The annual competition is announced through Serbian embassies and consulates — check with your national Serbian embassy for the current cycle.

title: Latvian State Scholarship
description: A scholarship for foreign students, researchers, and teaching staff at Latvian higher education institutions, funded by the Ministry of Education and Science.
eligibility: Citizenship of one of 39 eligible partner countries; bachelor's applicants need at least one completed academic year elsewhere.
prepResources: Check the current list of 39 eligible partner countries, since eligibility is restricted to citizens of those specific nations.

title: Croatian Government Bilateral Scholarships
description: Scholarships for foreign students, teachers, and researchers to study or conduct research at Croatian public institutions, via bilateral agreements or a Croatian-language study track.
eligibility: Citizenship of a country with a valid bilateral agreement with Croatia, such as Poland, Hungary, or Türkiye.
prepResources: Check whether your country currently holds an active bilateral education agreement with Croatia before applying.

title: EDUFI Fellowship
org: Government of Finland (Finnish National Agency for Education)
description: A Finnish government fellowship providing initial funding for foreign doctoral researchers to conduct thesis research at a Finnish university department.
eligibility: Open to all foreign nationals and fields of study, with a rolling deadline applied at least five months ahead of the intended start.
prepResources: The award is granted to a Finnish university department rather than directly to you — confirm the department's willingness to host you first.

title: Czech Government Scholarships for Developing Countries
description: A Czech government scholarship for students from developing countries to study master's or doctoral programs at Czech public universities, part of joint foreign development cooperation running since the 1950s.
eligibility: Must be officially recommended by eligible-country authorities such as embassies or ministries of education.
prepResources: Official recommendation from your home country's government is a required step before applying to the Czech program.

title: ICCR Scholarship Scheme
org: Government of India (Indian Council for Cultural Relations)
description: India's flagship cultural-diplomacy scholarship offering over 3,000 awards annually under 21 schemes to foreign students from roughly 180 countries.
eligibility: Applications only through ICCR's centralized Admissions to Alumni portal, requiring a 500-word English essay on an assigned topic.
prepResources: Choose the specific scheme (among 21) that matches your intended level and field of study before starting the A2A portal application.

title: Institute for Global Affairs Nonresident Fellowship (Independent America Program)
description: A one-year nonresident program for scholars and analysts working on rethinking US foreign policy for new global realities.
eligibility: Early-career scholars and analysts working on US foreign policy topics.
prepResources: A specific, well-argued foreign-policy thesis rather than a broad interest area is central to this nonresident fellowship's structure.

title: HIIA Future Leaders Program
description: A three-month in-person foreign policy research fellowship in Budapest for young international affairs professionals.
eligibility: Recent graduates under 30 with a degree in international relations, political science, history, or a related field; strong English required.
prepResources: Fellows must relocate to Budapest for the program's duration — plan for a three-month international stay when applying.

title: Bassem Sabry Democracy Fellowship
org: Tahrir Institute for Middle East Policy (TIMEP)
description: A six-month in-person fellowship in Washington DC for MENA-focused advocates of democratic, inclusive change, now in its tenth cohort.
eligibility: Bachelor's degree, under 35, based outside the US, three or more years of experience, must return home after the fellowship.
prepResources: This fellowship is delivered through Atlas Corps and includes full visa and relocation support — factor the required post-fellowship return commitment into your plans.

title: TIMEP Nonresident Fellowship
org: Tahrir Institute for Middle East Policy (TIMEP)
description: Supports MENA-based advocates, scholars, and analysts working on-the-ground for transparent, accountable governance.
eligibility: Advocates, scholars, or analysts based in or with strong ties to the MENA region.
prepResources: A track record of published or public analysis on MENA governance issues strengthens this nonresident fellowship application.

title: Stimson Center South Asia Program Junior Fellowship
description: A one-year, full-time Washington DC-based fellowship for early-career analysts researching South Asia security issues.
eligibility: Early-career analysts, scholars, or researchers interested in South Asia security.
prepResources: A specific South Asia security research interest, not a general regional-studies background, strengthens this competitive DC-based position.

title: Stimson Center South Asia Program Visiting Fellowship
description: A year-long research fellowship for India- and Pakistan-based analysts, combining ten months of research in the home country with one month in Washington DC, running since 1993.
eligibility: Analysts, scholars, or researchers from India and Pakistan.
prepResources: This fellowship has run continuously since 1993 — reviewing past visiting fellows' published work gives a strong sense of expected scope.

title: IISS Southeast Asian Young Leaders Programme Visiting Fellowship
description: Up to three months at the IISS Singapore office, including participation in the Shangri-La Dialogue and Young Leaders Programme.
eligibility: Early- to mid-career professionals in Southeast Asia's security and policy field.
prepResources: Participation in the Shangri-La Dialogue is a distinctive feature — highlight any prior regional security policy engagement in your application.

title: CEEW Global South Fellowship
org: Council on Energy, Environment and Water (CEEW)
description: A six- to twelve-month fellowship at Asia's leading climate and energy policy think tank in New Delhi for emerging climate leaders worldwide.
eligibility: PhD students in climate/sustainability, or postgraduates and professionals with three to four years of experience; open to all nationalities.
prepResources: This fellowship is unpaid but provides office space and initial housing help — plan your own funding source for the fellowship period.

title: Yale Jackson School Emerging Climate Leaders Fellowship
org: Yale Jackson School of Global Affairs
description: A five-month hybrid program for sixteen Global South climate and clean-energy practitioners, with sessions at Yale and in Paris.
eligibility: Young climate or clean-energy practitioners from the Global South.
prepResources: Existing hands-on experience in clean-energy or climate policy work (not just academic study) is central to this practitioner-focused fellowship.

title: Resolution Foundation Research Training Programme
description: A twelve-week fully funded, paid placement training economic and policy analysts, based in the Westminster office of a leading UK economic think tank.
eligibility: Degree-level qualification, from a Black, Asian, or other minority ethnic background, little prior experience required.
prepResources: No prior policy research experience is required — an interest in economic and social policy analysis is the key qualifying factor.

title: World Bank Robert S. McNamara Fellowships Program
description: An eight-month fellowship (September-May) matching developing-country researchers with World Bank research economists in Washington DC.
eligibility: Aspiring development economics researchers from developing countries.
prepResources: A clearly articulated development-economics research interest matched to an active World Bank research theme strengthens the application.

title: ACET Transformation Fellowship
description: Specialized fellowship tracks on structural change, productivity, gender, resilience, and evidence-based policymaking supporting ACET's research agenda.
eligibility: Early-career African economists or policy researchers.
prepResources: ACET's tracks are specific to structural economic transformation themes — align your proposal to one of the named tracks rather than a general economics topic.

title: Bruegel Visiting Fellowship
description: A flexible-duration research fellowship, from days to a year, for economists at a leading Brussels-based EU economic policy think tank.
eligibility: Researchers from academia, think tanks, or policy institutions with a strong economics background.
prepResources: Bruegel covers travel, accommodation, and related Brussels costs — propose a specific EU economic policy research question tied to Bruegel's current agenda.

title: Think Visegrad Fellowship
description: A six- to eight-week fellowship at Central European think tanks in Czechia, Hungary, Poland, or Slovakia, producing a policy paper.
eligibility: Expert fellows from non-Visegrad countries only; junior or mid-career researchers, past fellows ineligible to reapply.
prepResources: A minimum of four weeks must be spent in-person at the host think tank — plan your availability accordingly.

title: James Martin Center Visiting Fellows Program
org: James Martin Center for Nonproliferation Studies, Middlebury Institute
description: A nonproliferation and weapons-of-mass-destruction policy research fellowship based at the Middlebury Institute.
eligibility: Early- or mid-career nonproliferation researchers and analysts.
prepResources: A specific nonproliferation or arms-control research question, tied to the Center's existing program areas, strengthens the application.

title: IAPS AI Policy Fellowship
org: Institute for AI Policy and Strategy (IAPS)
description: A four-month remote-first AI policy research program with a mandatory two-week Washington DC residency.
eligibility: No technical AI background required and no prior policy experience needed; the DC track requires US work authorization.
prepResources: Strong analytical writing ability matters more than technical AI expertise — the fellowship trains policy skills from the ground up.

title: Chatham House Global Platform for Action Fellowship
description: A ten-month in-person London fellowship on humanitarian energy and climate/displacement policy, run with UNITAR and Oxford's Refugee-Led Research Hub.
eligibility: Bachelor's degree or equivalent, research experience, early-to-mid career from academia, NGO, government, or civil society.
prepResources: A clear connection between your background and humanitarian energy or displacement-related climate policy strengthens the application.

title: Chatham House Academy Fellowship
description: A ten-month in-person international affairs research fellowship in London, with four fellows selected per cohort.
eligibility: Bachelor's degree or equivalent, independent research experience, early-to-mid career.
prepResources: A well-defined independent research proposal in international affairs, not a general policy interest, is central to selection.

title: ODI Fellowship Scheme
description: A two-year fixed-term placement of young economists and statisticians as civil servants in finance ministries, central banks, and statistical offices in developing countries, running since 1963.
eligibility: Master's or PhD in economics, statistics, or a related field such as agricultural, environmental, or health economics; open to all nationalities.
prepResources: This is one of the longest-running development fellowship schemes in the world — reviewing past placements gives a strong sense of the government-facing role expected.
location: Africa, Asia, Caribbean, Pacific, Western Balkans

title: IIAS Asia in the World Fellowship Programme
description: A research fellowship for young and mid-career social sciences and humanities scholars studying Asia, based in Leiden.
eligibility: Preferably young or mid-career researchers in social sciences or humanities studying Asia.
prepResources: A specific Asia-focused research question grounded in social sciences or humanities (not STEM) fits this fellowship's scope.

title: SAIIA Africa Youth Portal and Research Programme
org: South African Institute of International Affairs (SAIIA)
description: A week-long workshop plus research programme training postgraduate youth researchers from Africa in policy communication.
eligibility: Postgraduate youth researchers from Africa.
prepResources: SAIIA covers travel and accommodation for selected participants — a clear postgraduate research background in policy-relevant fields strengthens the application.

title: SIT Furniture Design Award
description: A global furniture design award with an Emerging Furniture Designer category and a cash prize to help build a working prototype.
eligibility: Open to anyone aged 18 and older worldwide; entries must be no more than five years old; separate professional and student tracks.
prepResources: Submit both concept renderings and any existing prototype photos — the Innovation Center prize specifically funds building an actual prototype.

title: LAMP International Lighting Design Competition
description: An independent juried competition championing emerging lighting designers across professional, student, and manufactured-product categories.
eligibility: Open internationally with separate student and professional divisions.
prepResources: Strong lighting-specific renderings that show how the design interacts with a real space score better than pure product-shot images.

title: Dorothy Waxman International Textile Design Prize
description: An annual prize honoring sustainable, innovative textile design by current students or recent graduates, sponsored by the Mohawk Group.
eligibility: Enrolled textile, fashion, or interior design students and recent graduates worldwide.
prepResources: A clear sustainability narrative behind the material or process used is central to this prize's judging criteria.

title: One Found Sound Emerging Composer Award
description: An annual award supporting aspiring young composers, especially from historically excluded groups, culminating in a live orchestral premiere.
eligibility: Composition of no more than ten minutes, scored for a chamber orchestra of strings, woodwinds, brass, percussion, and harp.
prepResources: A recorded MIDI mockup or live recording of the piece is typically required alongside the score.

title: Berlin International Sound Design Competition
description: Sound designers create foley, sound effects, and dialogue for a supplied five-minute animated film.
eligibility: Open to sound designers of any age and nationality; registration fee applies.
prepResources: Working from the supplied animated film, a layered, dynamic sound mix that responds to on-screen action is what judges evaluate.

title: Carouge International Ceramics Competition
org: City of Carouge / Musée de Carouge
description: A biennial international ceramics competition running since 1987, with exhibited works shown at the Musée de Carouge.
eligibility: Open to all ceramic artists internationally.
prepResources: Since exhibited works are physically displayed, factor shipping logistics for finalist pieces into your planning.

title: Chrysalis Award
description: Supports emerging US ceramic artists with funding, an exhibition opportunity, and organizational membership.
eligibility: Emerging ceramic artists based in the United States.
prepResources: A cohesive body of recent ceramic work (not a single strong piece) is what this award evaluates for emerging-artist support.
