title: AR Emerging Architecture Awards
description: A long-running award (since 1999) recognizing up-and-coming architecture, urban design, and landscape practices, with several past winners now internationally renowned.
eligibility: Practice founders must be born after January 1, 1981; individuals working independently are also eligible.
prepResources: A built or substantially developed project (not just a concept) is expected, given this award's focus on emerging practices already producing real work.

title: Australian Furniture Design Award (AFDA)
description: One of Australia's richest design prizes for a furniture concept, with a commercialization opportunity through manufacturer Stylecraft.
eligibility: Open to Australian designers only.
prepResources: Shortlisted Stage One entrants receive an honorarium for building a prototype — plan for a two-stage submission process.

title: SoundTrack_Cologne European Talent Competition
description: A sound design and composition talent competition awarding a professional 5.1 cinematic mixdown session in a prestigious studio.
eligibility: Emerging composers or sound designers, open internationally.
prepResources: A composition or sound design portfolio piece with cinematic scope, not a short jingle, best fits this competition's studio-mixdown prize.

title: Illustraciencia International Illustration Award
description: An award for scientific and naturalist illustration promoting the role of visual art in science communication, run by Spain's national research council.
eligibility: Open worldwide to individuals or teams aged 18 and older; one original illustration per entrant; no entry fee.
prepResources: Accuracy of the scientific subject matter is judged alongside artistic quality — cross-check technical details with a relevant scientific source.

title: World Illustration Awards
org: Association of Illustrators / Directory of Illustration
description: A major illustration award split into New Talent and Professional groups across categories like advertising, editorial, and books.
eligibility: Open internationally; the New Talent category is specifically for students and early-career illustrators.
prepResources: Enter the New Talent category if you're a student or recent graduate — it's judged separately from the Professional track.

title: Global Comic Awards
description: An international manga, comic, and webtoon competition spotlighting emerging and established creators around an annual theme.
eligibility: Open worldwide across an Open Category and a themed Category.
prepResources: Choose between the Open Category and the year's specific Theme Category based on which better fits your existing work.

title: AMBERIF Design Award
description: An international competition promoting Baltic amber as a medium for artistic jewelry expression, tied to the Amberif trade fair.
eligibility: Open internationally to jewelry designers and artists working with amber.
prepResources: A design that specifically showcases amber's unique material properties (rather than treating it as a generic gemstone) fits the competition's mandate.

title: Singapore Jewellery Design Award (SJDA)
description: An international jewelry design award with a dedicated category for students and entrants with under five years of industry experience.
eligibility: Design students and new entrants with under five years of professional experience.
prepResources: The grand prize includes travel to a major industry trade fair — factor this networking opportunity into your submission's professional framing.

title: GIT World Jewelry Design Awards
description: An international jewelry design award requiring finalists to produce actual pieces with sponsor support before a judging panel in Thailand.
eligibility: Open internationally; winners must attend the ceremony in Thailand to claim the prize.
prepResources: Finalists work with sponsor materials to produce a physical piece — plan for travel to Thailand if selected as a finalist.

title: Pro Carton Young Designers Award
description: A free-to-enter packaging design award focused exclusively on cartonboard, for European design students.
eligibility: Students enrolled at any European school or university; free entry.
prepResources: A design that solves a genuine packaging problem using cartonboard's specific structural properties fits this award's brief best.

title: Starpack Students Competition
description: A UK-based real-world metal packaging design challenge for design students, with a sponsored brief specific to metal packaging.
eligibility: Open to design students, entering a sponsored metal-packaging design brief.
prepResources: Address the specific sponsored brief closely, since this competition judges against a real industry-set challenge, not an open theme.

title: AICC Student Packaging Design Competition
description: A US packaging design competition with structural and graphic design categories, including an all-expenses-paid industry trip for winners.
eligibility: Team-based student entries.
prepResources: Choose between the structural and graphic design categories based on your team's strengths, since judging criteria differ between them.

title: Joffrey Ballet Winning Works Choreographic Competition
description: A long-running choreographic competition giving winners a stipend, rehearsal time, and a company residency to create new work.
eligibility: Open to choreographers; five winners selected annually.
prepResources: A video of prior choreographic work performed by a company (not just a solo demo reel) strengthens the application.

title: League of Composers/ISCM Steven R. Gerber Composition Prize
description: An annual composition competition recognizing outstanding new concert works, with a possible New York performance for the winner.
eligibility: Open to composers submitting original scores.
prepResources: A recent, fully notated score in concert-music format (not a film-score excerpt) fits this competition's traditional new-music focus.
location: New York, USA (international submissions typically accepted)
